feat.name: Embrace Death
entries: There are some amongst the Annukara that channel their inherited powers and shape themselves to better fit their progenitor. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain resistance to necrotic damage.

You can cast the {@spell hex} spell as a 1st-level spell using this feat, except it targets both Strength and Dexterity ability checks, instead of one ability score of your choice. You regain the ability to cast this spell when you finish a long rest, or if you die and are brought back to life. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

feat.name: Tenebrous Protector
entries: Sometimes the fiercest Annukara manifest their heritage and power in a protective aura devoted to being a guardian to their allies, and a menace to all that would harm them. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you lose hit points from Shared Burden, you can gain temporary hit points equal to the hit points you lost. These hit points last for 1 minute.

When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack while you have temporary hit points from this feat, you can lose a number of those hit points up to your proficiency bonus to deal extra necrotic damage equal to the number lost.

As an action, you can manifest a fierce aura of steadfast, dedicated protection for 1 minute. For the duration, you can add any temporary hit points you gain from this feat together, and when you use Shared Burden on a creature, you can use it on that creature without a reaction until the start of your next turn, but only once per instance of damage. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Three Tailed
entries: Through great focus and meditation, you have learned to manifest additional tails, which grant you latent magic abilities. Choose up to three 1st-level spells from any classes. The spells must be enchantment or illusion spells, and must have a casting time of 1 action. You can cast each of the chosen spells once with this ability. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you inish a long rest.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

feat.name: Lost Love
entries: The devotion and love of a dajix is often matched only by the power of its mourning. You gain the following benefits:

items: When a creature you're {@condition charmed} by dies, you can unleash a wail of anguish. Each creature within 15 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or take psychic damage equal to your level + your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

feat.name: Dragon's Senses
entries: You have inherited the keen eyes of dragonkind. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill. If you already have proficiency, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make with it.

You can take the Search action at the end of another creature's turn (no action required by you), but only to make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check. You can't do so again until the end of your next turn.

feat.name: Immutable
entries: Only you are in control of your form, and your mind's shield is impenetrable. You gain the following benefits:

items: You are immune to any spell or effect that would alter your form, unless you wish to be affected by it.
When you use your Blessing of the Ambiguous One feature, the duration of the effect is extended to 8 hours, and your memories can't be altered or erased for the same duration.

feat.name: Soul of Defiance
entries: From the days of the one who freed your kin, a residing promise and spirit of freedom has existed throughout many pseudodragons. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain one additional use of your Ancient Power trait.

While under the effects of your Nostalgic Form, you have advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects, and you can instead expend a use of your Ancient Power trait to reroll saving throws against such effects.

If you succeed on a saving throw to resist being {@condition grappled}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition restrained}, or {@condition stunned}, you can choose a number of creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you up to your proficiency bonus that failed the same saving throw. The effect ends for them.

feat.name: Elder's Ward
entries: The ward of the elder Yuan-Ti and your predecessors manifests every so often. Through continuous study, you have learned to repurpose and utilize it yourself. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you see a creature within 120 feet of you target you or an area you are in with a spell, you know what level the spell is.
When you would fail a saving throw against a spell, you can reroll the saving throw. You must use the new roll. You can do so twice, and regain both uses when you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Inferno Heart
entries: The inferno burning in your heart flows wilder than the greatest of wildfires, and through concentration it has come under your control. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can communicate telepathically with any creature that has innate fire resistance or immunity, while it is within 60 feet of you. The creature can understand you and can respond telepathically.

When a hostile creature forces you to make a saving throw to avoid fire damage, you can use your reaction to weave the flames aside, and reduce the damage you take by half. When you do, you can cause a creature within 10 feet of you to take fire damage equal to the amount you reduced. You can't cause a creature to take fire damage this way if it's subject to the same effect.

As an action, you can reach into the fire in your heart, manipulating it from the core of your being into the world around you for 1 minute. As an action on each of your turns, including when you activate this effect, you can send forth flames in a blazing trail. The area of the trail begins in a space within 5 feet of you and consists of up to six 5-foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@dice 3d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Planar Mastery
entries: You can utilize your connection with the cosmos in battle, and to ensure you are always aware of your planar surroundings. You gain the following benefits:

items: You always know what plane you're on.
You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would forcefully teleport you, or send you to a different plane of existence.

As a bonus action, you can conjure a portal that teleports you up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, wreathing you in force energy. After teleporting, your next attack before the end of your next turn deals an additional {@dice 1d6} force damage. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Revealing Light
entries: Your holy light is potent, assisting you in revealing all. You gain the following benefits:

items: While an object affected by your {@spell light} cantrip is within 5 feet of a creature, object, or area affected by an illusion spell, the DC to discern the illusion is reduced by your Charisma modifier.

As an action, you can create a magical burst of radiance from your hand that blinds your foes. Hostile creatures within 5 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be {@condition blinded} until the start of your next turn. Creatures that fail this saving throw can't benefit from being invisible for the same duration.

feat.name: The Flame of Contradiction
entries: The blood of the Archdevil of Contradictions runs cold through your hellfire veins. You gain the following benefits:

items: As an action, you can use your {@spell mage hand} cantrip to grip a target within 5 feet of it. Make a melee spell attack using your Charisma modifier. On a hit, the target is gripped by the hand. While the target is gripped, it takes {@dice 1d4} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. A target can attempt to break the hand's grip as an action (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier), ending the effect on a success.
When you deal damage with a spell from your Innate Spellcasting trait, you deal additional damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
You can change any fire damage you deal to cold damage.

feat.name: Empty Heart
entries: The emptiness in your heart is something that you have devoted yourself to resolving, and while such a thing might not yet be accomplished, you have learned to manifest that emptiness as a part of yourself. As an action, you can cause yourself to become unidentifiable, gaining the appearance of a vaguely humanoid silhouette. This effect lasts until you dismiss it as a bonus action.
While you're unidentifiable, creatures hear your voice as their voice, you leave no tracks or other traces of your passage, and you have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.

feat.name: Portal Prophet
entries: Your connections to the greater cosmos are enhanced at large. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you're meditating, as with Familiarity with the Cosmos or Breath of the Wild, you can't be surprised, and you know the location of any creature within 30 feet of you that isn't behind total cover.

As an action, you can cause portals to blink in and out of existence in a 10-foot radius sphere centered on a point you can see within 30 feet of you for 1 minute. Any non-magical ranged attack made against a creature in the area misses, you can expend 5 feet of your movement while in the area to teleport to any unoccupied space in the area, and you can use your bonus action to teleport a creature you can see in the area to any other unoccupied space on the ground in the area. An unwilling creature that succeeds on a Charisma saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier) is unaffected. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Antecedents' Teachings
entries: The teaching of your forebears is oft glimpsed into. Through continuous glimpses, you have expanded your skillset, and become more reliable at peeking into the experiences of your ancestors. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice from {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Stealth}, and {@skill Survival}.
If you roll the number on your Ancestral Glimpse on an ability check that uses either of those skills, the trait triggers without expending a use of it.

feat.name: Spiritual Guidance
entries: The connection with your ancestors is deeply ingrained in your soul, not only making you more in touch with your ancestors, but also extending their help to your allies. You roll three times at the end of a long rest for your Ancestral Glimpse racial trait, instead of once. You can use each result only once.
Additionally, you can use a glimpse when an ally you can see rolls the number on the glimpse on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.

feat.name: Nerthulian Magic
entries: Your inherent leviathan nature calls, coming forth in the form of magical essence. You learn the {@spell shape water|XGE} cantrip.

In addition, you learn the {@spell crashing wave|TheDarkAscension} and {@spell swim through space|TheDarkAscension} spells, which you can cast at the lowest level for each. Once you cast either spell, you must finish a long rest before you can cast that spell again using this feat. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

feat.name: Warrior of the Deep
entries: You're blessed by the great deity of the sea, which sharpens your skill with traditional nerthulian weaponry. You gain the following benefits:

items: As a bonus action when you hit a creature within 20 feet of you with a ranged weapon attack using a javelin, spear, or trident, you can move up to half your speed. If you end this movement within 5 feet of the creature you hit, you can retrieve the weapon you attacked with and use it to make a melee weapon attack against the creature.

When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack using a javelin, spear, or trident, you can impale it, which causes the weapon to stay lodged in its body until the weapon is removed as an action. While the creature is impaled this way, you have advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to attempt to grapple it. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Watery Warlord
entries: You've been hardened on the battlefield, and can lead even during the cruelest conflict. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can cause your voice to be heard up to 300 feet away.
When you use your Tactical Mind, you can instead cause your initiative to become equal to the target's initiative, or cause the target's initiative to become equal to your own.
While you're within 5 feet of a hostile creature, you can cause it to have disadvantage on opportunity attacks against creatures other than you.

feat.name: Crow's Insight
entries: Your progenitor blesses you with magical insight, giving you a deeper inherent synergy with magical items. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can bypass any racial prerequisites for magic items.

As a bonus action, you can increase the attack, damage, or AC bonus of one magic item in your possession of your choice by 1. Only one magic item can benefit from this feature at a time, and only you benefit from it.

As an action, you can end attunement to a magic item you are attuned to within 100 feet of you. If you are holding or wearing a magic item that requires attunement, you can attune to it as part of the same action. You can't end attunement again in this way until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Blood of the Ordning
entries: The blood of ancient giants flows through your veins, giving you immense power and allowing you to surpass your mortal size to closer match your ancestors. You gain an additional use of Stone's Endurance.

Additionally, you can use an action to grow in size, using the blood of the Ordning within you. For 1 minute, you are under the enlarge effects of the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell (no concentration required). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Braveheart
entries: As a creature that inherently looks up to the great dragons, you have sought to better yourself, to one day be a good representation of them. You gain two additional uses of your Heart of the Dragon feature.

If an ally that can hear you within 30 feet of you would fail a saving throw, you can expend a use of Heart of the Dragon as a reaction to have them reroll the saving throw. If the ally succeeds on the new roll, they have advantage on the next attack roll or saving throw they make before the end of their next turn.

feat.name: Breath of Ancients
entries: The breath of those you take after is empowered, the glands growing even stronger through use and training. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your Breath Weapon's damage dice are doubled when you gain this feat. When your Breath Weapon gains an additional damage die, it instead gains two additional damage dice.
If your Breath Weapon is a cone, you can double its distance when you use it. If your Breath Weapon is a line, you can double its length when you use it.
If you take damage from a hostile dragon's breath weapon, you regain a use of your Breath Weapon. You can't regain a use of it in this way again until you complete a long rest.

feat.name: Draconic Incorporation
entries: Having practiced draconic forms, through rigorous focus and perpetual trials, you have managed to incorporate aspects of dragons into yourself. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you use your Change Appearance trait, you can manifest draconic properties to enhance your form, gaining one of the following features until you change it: claws, eyes, or wings.

{@b Claws.} Your claws become natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike. If you strike with two free hands, the {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}.
{@b Eyes.} You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks based on sight.
{@b Wings.} You have a flying speed of 30 feet, which can't be used to ascend. When you end one of your turns flying in the air, you descend by 5 feet.

feat.name: Fallen Angel
entries: With your connection to your celestial heritage absent, all that has been left in its place is a well of terrifying necrotic power for you to tap into. You gain the following benefits:

items: Creatures can't have advantage on saving throws made to resist being frightened by you.

As an action, you can expend your Healing Hands feature to pulse necrotic energies into a creature you touch. The creature takes necrotic damage equal to your level + your Charisma modifier, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage you deal.

During Necrotic Shroud, you can repeat the effect causing creatures within 10 feet of you to be frightened as a bonus action on each of your turns. If a creature succeeds its saving throw, it can't be affected again until 24 hours pass.

feat.name: Hakuna Matata
entries: Growing up, you were always free from the worries and stress of life, a habit and mindset that carried into adulthood. You gain the following benefits:

items: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}. If you already have advantage, or gain advantage from another source, you can reroll one of the dice once when making the saving throw.
Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using brewer's tools, as well as the gaming set and instrument you chose with your Worry Free feature.

If a creature is {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or suffering from madness within 60 feet of you, you can sing a song to soothe them over the course of 1 minute. At the end of the minute, the creature can repeat the saving throw against the effect with advantage. Regardless of success or failure, the target can't benefit from this feature again until it benefits from a long rest.

feat.name: Karma Chameleon
entries: Having learned how to hunt for your companions growing up, you learned to take upon different aspects of your lizardly heritage, specifically focused upon hunting and camouflage. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your climb speed increases by 10 feet.
You can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check. While you aren't moving, you don't need your hands to remain on a surface.

If you remain pressed against a solid surface without moving or taking actions for 1 minute, you can cause your skin to naturally adapt to your surroundings. Until you move or take an action or reaction, you are {@condition invisible}.

feat.name: Relentless Crusader
entries: The depth of your anger for mortal evil is all-consuming, such so that you pull out even greater power from within you, but it need not consume your allies. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can cast the {@spell bane} spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest. You are aware of the location of creatures affected by the spell within 30 feet of you when cast using this feat, even if they are hidden or {@condition invisible}. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.
When you activate Radiant Consumption, you can choose creatures other than yourself to not be affected by the radiant damage it deals at the end of each of your turns.
You can unattune to cursed magic items without suffering the effects of the curse.

feat.name: Scales of the Vrael
entries: You have learned to harness the blood of your favored ancestors, to morph your own form into that of a snake's. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can speak with snakes, as with the {@spell speak with animals} spell.

As an action, you can assume the form of a {@creature giant poisonous snake}. While in this shape, you follow the rules for wild shape, except you retain your current hit points, hit dice, and hit point maximum, you retain your proficiency bonus, and you can still speak and cast spells that don't have material components. The DC to resist the poison damage becomes 12 + your proficiency bonus. This form lasts until you exit the form as an action.

feat.name: The Queen's Wings
entries: The blood of Glasya, Queen of the Erinyes, flows through your veins and blesses you with wings to fly. When you gain this feat, you can choose to have either the feathery wings of an {@creature erinyes}, or the leathery wings of an incubus or {@creature succubus}. You can sprout these wings from your back as an action or a bonus action, and they last until you cause them to fold into your back as an action or a bonus action. While you have these wings, you detect as a fiend, and you have a flying speed of 30 feet, which you can only use while you aren't wearing heavy armor.

If you are {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained}, you can extend these wings as an action - sprouting them if they weren't already present - to break free of your confines, immediately ending all effects causing you to be {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained}. When you do so, creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you are pushed up to 10 feet away from you. You can't use your wings in this way again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Vibrant Guardian
entries: You have tapped into your celestial ancestry, furthering the depth of your holy powers. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can cast the {@spell bless} spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.
The flying speed of your Radiant Soul feature is increased to 60 feet.
During Radiant Soul, you are also under the effects of the {@spell protection from evil and good} spell (no concentration required by you).

feat.name: Wellspring of Vitality
entries: The latent power of the Spring of Youth from your ancestors' Plane flows through your veins once more. Your hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your hit point maximum increases by an additional 1 hit point.
Additionally, you can cast the {@spell aid} spell, targeting up to five allies of your choice when cast using this feat, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Winds of Fury
entries: The winds are your ally, carrying you and your strikes to the destination you ordain. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your walking speed increases by 5 feet.
Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature within 60 feet of you, you can push it up to 10 feet away from you, or pull it up to 10 feet towards you.

You can cast the {@spell warding wind} spell as an action, or as a reaction after you roll initiative, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest. When cast this way, your ranged weapon attacks don't have disadvantage if the attack passes out of the wind. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for the spell.

feat.name: Expanded Knowledge
entries: You have honed your mind and your patience, expanding your capacity for knowledge. You gain the following benefits:

items: If you have levels in a class with a Spells Known column on its class table, you know an additional three spells from your class spell list. If you have levels in a class with spells that you prepare, you can prepare an additional three spells, and must spend additional time preparing them as normal for your class. If you have more than one class, and more than one of them fulfills the previously stated criteria, this applies to one class of your choice.

feat.name: Indefatigable
entries: The test of endurance is one that is practically a pastime for you. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you can't have disadvantage on ability checks from {@condition exhaustion}.
You have advantage on saving throws made to resist {@condition exhaustion}.
The DC to avoid a level of {@condition exhaustion} after 24 hours without a long rest increases by 2 for every 24 hours after, instead of increasing by 5.

feat.name: Morning Person
entries: Waking up is the start of a new day. For many, the start of the day can be the worst, but for you the beginning of the day is when you shine brightest. When you finish a long rest, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to your level + your Constitution modifier.
You recover an additional level of {@condition exhaustion}.
You gain a {@dice d6} die that you can add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw you make within 1 hour.

feat.name: One Weapon Master
entries: You master wielding a weapon with a single hand. While you have at least one hand free, you gain the following benefits:

items: When you attack using a versatile weapon you're wielding with one hand, you deal damage as if you were wielding it with two hands.
When you attack a creature within 5 feet of you using a melee weapon you're wielding with one hand, you can use a bonus action to pry the creature's guard open. You gain advantage on the attack roll.
If you're disarmed of a weapon you're holding, you can use a reaction to immediately pick it up or to immediately draw another weapon.

feat.name: Tool Specialist
entries: Persistently sticking with a favored set of tools enables you insight into how to use them to their fullest extent. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in one type of {@filter artisan's tools|items|type=artisan's tools} of your choice.
Choose one type of artisan's tools with which you have proficiency. You add double your proficiency bonus for any ability check you make with it.

feat.name: Shield Surfer
entries: A fun pastime turned into a combat tactic, you have mastered using a shield for mobility. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency with {@item shield|phb|shields}.
You can don or doff shields as a bonus action.

If you move 10 feet or more in a straight line while holding a shield or a similarly shaped object, you can slide on it in a straight line without expending movement or provoking opportunity attacks. You can slide up to 10 feet for each 10 feet you moved. The distance you can slide is doubled if you slide downhill, and halved if you slide uphill.

feat.name: Thing of Legends
entries: The tales woven about you can be made of myths and legends, like standing strong in a dragon's breath and surviving, or being blasted by the ray of a beholder without turning to dust, but most importantly, these tales are sometimes true.
When you would fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Stalwart
entries: You stand tall, resilient, stalwart in the face of that which would sway, defeat, or cripple lesser people. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you gain this feat, choose one of the following conditions: {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition poisoned}. You have advantage on saving throws made to resist the chosen condition.

entries.name: Age
entries: Durumir mature at the age of 35, and can live to be 450 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: As the paragons of that which stands righteous, that which other races look up to, most would expect the Durumir to tend towards lawful good. In fact, they are more often than not lawful neutral; obeying the laws of the land but protecting nature, by whatever means necessary.

entries.name: Size
entries: Durumir are often found to stand between 6 and 7 feet tall, weighing anywhere between 140 to 190 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: The Fruit of Knowledge
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Nature} skill.

entries.name: Mask of the Wild
entries: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

entries.name: Familiarity with the Cosmos
entries: By spending 1 hour uninterrupted in meditation, you can learn the general direction of the closest portal within 1 mile, along with which plane the portal is connected to, and can sense it until your next long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: As a denizen of the forest, your habitat was visited often by the Fey. You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: Four subraces populate the forests; Dáinn, Duneyrr, Duraþrór and Dvalinn.

entries.name: Will of the Dragonbane
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

entries.name: Wrath of the Serpent
entries: When you hit with an attack against a creature, you can reduce that creature's fly speed to 0 until the end of its next turn. You can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Blessing of the Tree
entries: You can add your Constitution modifier to death saving throws.

entries.name: Mighty Legs
entries: When you fall and aren't incapacitated, you can subtract up to 50 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage.

entries.name: Delay the Inveitable
entries: When hit by a weapon attack that isn't a critical hit, you can use your reaction to have the attack miss instead. The creature that attacked you can't miss with its next attack against you within the next minute. You can't do this again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Thine Prophecy Foretold
entries: If you or an ally within 60 feet of you is surprised, you can choose one ally. They can't be surprised this round. You can't do this again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Breath of the Wild
entries: You can spend 10 minutes with your eyes closed in meditation, at the end of which you gain blindsight out to 60 feet, are incapacitated, and cannot move. If you are in a forest, you gain blindsight out to 120 feet instead and also become invisible. You can end these effects at will.

entries.name: Fleet of Hoof
entries: Your walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Age
entries: Mothfolk are born as larvae, and are only considered fully grown after metamorphosis. They are considered adults once their wings are fully grown, and live into their mid-forties on average. The oldest mothfolk live until their late fifties.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Mothfolk are slow to anger, yet quick to become impassioned about the things they find important, and they are good at making the most out of life. They lean toward chaotic alignments, and away from evil ones.

entries.name: Size
entries: Mothfolk range from a bit under six feet to almost seven feet tall, not counting their lengthy antennae. Their builds tend towards the light and lithe, and they usually weigh between 130 and 160 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Compound Vision
entries: Your compound eyes are excellent at finding what little light there is in darkness. You can see in dim light as if it were bright light out to 120 feet, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can discern colors in dim light and darkness, but only on the ultraviolet spectrum.

entries.name: Wings
entries: As a mothkin, you have large, cloak-like wings. You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed, which can't be used to ascend. When you end one of your turns flying in the air, you descend by 5 feet.

entries.name: Armed and Dangerous
entries: Mothfolk have four arms, and each of them can hold an object as normal. You don't benefit from a shield if you're holding a weapon with the two-handed property or more than one weapon. You can interact with a second object or feature of the environment for free during your turn.

entries.name: Natural Camouflage
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Ivathi. Ivathi is a language native to mothfolk. It is learned and internalized as a larvae and spoken upon emergence. The language is complex and almost impossible to speak with a human mouth, as mothfolk speak with a specialized organ in their throat that other beings lack. Ivathi sounds like a series of squishes, clicks, and chitters, cut with the occasional thrumming or soft vowel. It uses its own alphabet, and translating it to other scripts only results in an approximation of the language's true sound.
If you know Ivathi, you can also communicate very simple ideas to moths, and understand similar ideas from moths that choose to share them with you.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: All mothfolk share a common ancestor, but there are three distinct groups of mothfolk that roam the world, each with their own customs and abilities. Most live in secluded communities, but some have chosen to engage in the world society. Choose one of these subraces.

entries.name: Close to Nature
entries: Pick one {@filter cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list|spells|level=0|class=Druid}. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Soothing Appearance
entries: You have advantage on {@skill Animal Handling} checks to calm hostile or agitated animals.

entries.name: Epitome of Grace
entries: Your broad wingspan means that you are always under the effects of a {@spell feather fall} spell.

entries.name: Hardened Carapace
entries: When you aren't wearing armor, you have a natural AC of 14 + your Dexterity modifier (to a maximum of 2). You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC.

entries.name: A Warrior's Upbringing
entries: You have proficiency with the spear, trident and war pick, as well as one simple or martial weapon of your choice.

entries.name: Extra Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write two extra languages of your choice.

entries.name: High Class
entries: You can double your proficiency bonus for Charisma checks when speaking with noble or high-class creatures.

entries.name: Well-Mannered
entries: Whenever you make an ability check with a Charisma-based skill you are proficient in, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 4 or lower as a 5.

entries.name: Age
entries: Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10-year-old human child by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Dragonborn tend to extremes, with chromatic dragonborn taking on their ancestor's cruelty and malice, while metallic dragonborn follow ideals of benevolence and justice.

entries.name: Size
entries: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation.

When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes {@dice 2d6} damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases to {@dice 3d6} at 6th level, {@dice 4d6} at 11th level, and {@dice 5d6} at 16th level.
After you use your breath weapon, you can't use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: You have tough, scaly skin. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 14 + your Dexterity modifier (max 2). You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Draconic is thought to be one of the oldest languages and is often used in the study of magic. The language sounds harsh to most other creatures and includes numerous hard consonants and sibilants.

entries.name: Color
entries: In the same manner as true dragons, dragonborn are divided into a number of different colors. These colors fall into two categories, the chromatics — black, blue, green, red, and white — and the metallics — brass, bronze, copper, gold, and silver.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals acid damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to acid damage.

entries.name: Brutal and Cruel
entries: You add your Constitution modifier to the damage of your breath weapon.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals lightning damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to lightning damage.

entries.name: Desert Predator
entries: As an action, you can burrow yourself into sand or other loose soil. Creatures that attempt to detect you take a -5 penalty to their Wisdom (Perception) checks as long as you remain there without moving or taking actions.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals fire damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Boldly Talkative
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals lightning damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to lightning damage.

entries.name: Dragon of the Coast
entries: You have a swim speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals acid damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to acid damage.

entries.name: Good Host
entries: You have proficiency in {@skill Performance}, and can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals fire damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Reserved Companion
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Insight} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals poison damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Constitution saving throw. You have resistance to poison damage.

entries.name: Manipulative Schemer
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals fire damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Arrogant Tyrant
entries: You have advantage on saving throws to avoid being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals cold damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Constitution saving throw. You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Hoarding History
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill History} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals cold damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Constitution saving throw. You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Primal and Vengeful
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Survival} skill.

entries.name: Age
entries: Kobolds reach adulthood at age 6 and can live up to 120 years, but rarely do so.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Kobolds are fundamentally selfish, making most of them evil, but their reliance on the strength of their group makes them trend toward law.

entries.name: Size
entries: Kobold are between 2 and 3 feet tall and weigh between 25 and 35 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern colour in darkness, only shades of grey.

entries.name: Distraction
entries: As an action on your turn, you can distract a nearby foe. Until the end of your next turn, your allies gain advantage on attack rolls against a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you.
Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Natural Trapper
entries: You are proficient in thieves tools, and whenever you make an ability check to create traps (found on page 84 of Xanathar's Guide to Everything), you can add double your proficiency bonus to the check, rather than your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Heart of the Dragon
entries: When you succeed on a saving throw, you can have the next attack roll or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn have advantage.
Once you have used this trait, you cannot do so again until you complete a short or long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nerthulians mature at 21, and can live to be 250 years old, though ones that live to be over 150 are rare, more often than not finding death on the battlefield.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The militaristic nature of their society will almost always lead to one of two things: Creatures tending towards law, or chaos. To be neutral is almost unheard of.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nerthulians often stand between 6 and 7 feet tall in adulthood. As they become older, they can reach 8 feet tall, at which point they cease growing. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Depth Dweller
entries: You can breathe both air and water, and you ignore any of the drawbacks caused by a deep, underwater environment.

entries.name: Deepsight
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray. If you are underwater, you can instead see in dim light and darkness within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light.

entries.name: Nerthulian Weapon Training
entries: You have proficiency with the javelin, spear, trident, and net.

entries.name: Tactical Mind
entries: When you roll initiative, you can swap your initiative and the initiative of a willing creature you can see.

entries.name: Wave Cutter
entries: You can use your action to shoot a stream of water from your mouth, sharp enough to pierce steel, which forms a line 30 feet long and 1 foot wide. All creatures in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Strength modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, a target takes piercing damage equal to your Strength modifier x 4, and the next attack roll made against it before the end of your next turn has advantage. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: Your aquatic heritage lends you the knowledge of the tongue spoken by the leviathans. You can speak, read, and write Common and Aquan.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tegokka often reach adulthood by 10 years of age, as they mature quickly. While most tegokka reach the end of their life by the time they're 80, some manage to live to the old age of 110.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Many tegokka are strong believers in the natural forces of the world, and believe perfect balance can be achieved if nothing is tampered with. As such, most tegokka are true neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tegokka are short, standing only between 2 and 3 feet tall, and weigh around 25 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Adhesive Skin
entries: You have a climbing speed of 30 feet, and you don't suffer disadvantage on ability checks you make while climbing a slippery or wet surface.

entries.name: Forest Resilience
entries: You're used to the natural dangers of a forest environment. You have advantage on saving throws against diseases.

entries.name: Handy Tail
entries: When you take the Use an Object action, you can use your tail to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. Your tail can't carry more than 10 pounds.

entries.name: Shed Skin
entries: As a bonus action, you can shed your outer most layer of skin. You immediately escape from any grapple, moving to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. In addition, if you are restrained or poisoned, those conditions immediately end.
Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Worry Free
entries: You gain proficiency in brewer's tools, as well as one gaming set and one instrument of your choice.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can read and write Common and one other language of your choice. Most tegokka often learn Elvish, Gnomish, or Halfling, as those languages reflect the races the tegokka deal with the most.

entries.name: Age
entries: Ventiam mature at 85 years old, and can live to be 900.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The Ventiam are an incredibly neutral people, often not feeling one way or the other so long as they further their own research. Some tend towards the creation of that which will help the forces of good, and some tend towards imbuing their creations with the dark arts.

entries.name: Size
entries: The Ventiam were not created with any care for their height, and so are not incredibly tall. They are usually between 5 and a half feet to 6 and a half feet tall, and can weigh between 140 to 190 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Ease of Descent
entries: You can cast the {@spell feather fall} at will, targeting only yourself.

entries.name: Gale Force
entries: As a bonus action, you can launch yourself up to 60 feet in any direction. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Knowledge of the Ancients
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill. Additionally, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Intelligence (Arcana) checks and Intelligence (History) checks made to determine the origin or power of a magical item.

entries.name: Denizens of the Sky
entries: You're acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet. You're also naturally adapted to cold climates, as described in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.

entries.name: Languages
entries: Your grand knowledge and long times of research gives you a deep insight into the dialects of other languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and up to 2 other languages of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Annukara mature at 7 years old, and can live to be 65 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The Annukara often do not follow laws, and tend towards a naturally chaotic state amongst their nomadic tribes, with only a single unifying factor; protecting the young.

entries.name: Size
entries: The Annukara are relatively above average in height, and so are not at the same level as Nerthulians, but are still quite up there. They are usually around 6 feet to 6 and a half feet tall, and can weigh between 180 to 250 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Heartsense
entries: While the skin of your palms are in contact with a creature, you can detect the heartbeat of that creature and any other creatures in physical contact with it. You have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks against a living creature you can sense in this way. An unwilling creature must be grappled or restrained for you to use this trait on it.

entries.name: Desert Wanderers
entries: You're acclimated to harsh deserts. You're also naturally adapted to hot climates and extreme heat, as described in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.

entries.name: Quadrupedal Instinct
entries: If you have both hands free, your movement speed is not halved while prone, and it only costs you 10 feet of movement to stand from prone.

entries.name: Shared Burden
entries: When a creature within 5 feet of you takes damage, you can use a reaction to reduce the damage it takes by your proficiency bonus. You then lose the same number of hit points.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You hold a heritage of darkness within you, one which helps you speak to the depths. You can speak, read, and write Common and Abyssal.

entries.name: Age
entries: Pseudodragons mature at 14 years old, and reach their peak at around 120 years old. At 200 years old, they can hold onto life for as long as they deem necessary before passing on, but they invariably retire as their bodies can longer sustain the lifestyle they otherwise may have.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: With the spirit of freedom ingrained in their history, pseudodragons tend towards chaotic alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Pseudodragons are generally longer than they are tall due to the fact that they are often quadrupeds. They are around 3 to 4 feet in length, and weigh around 40 to 60 lbs. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 25 feet, and you have a flying speed of 30 feet. If you end your turn in the air with nothing to hold you aloft, you fall.

entries.name: Draconic Nature
entries: Your creature type is dragon, rather than humanoid.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Ancient Power
entries: As a reaction when you would make a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, you can gain advantage on the roll. Once you use this ability, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Draconic Awareness
entries: As a bonus action, you can gain blindsight out to 30 feet until the start of your next turn. Once you use this ability, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Nostalgic Form
entries: By spending 1 minute in concentration, you can make yourself Tiny as though under the reduce effects of the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell. While Tiny, you cannot wield weapons nor hold anything heavier than 10 lbs, but your wings can now support your weight. You do not fall at the end of your turn if you are in the air with nothing holding you aloft. You must maintain a deep concentration on this form to maintain it. Engaging in combat, or taking damage, will immediately end it. You can also end it at will (no action required).

entries.name: Languages
entries: The draconic energy flowing through your mind enables you great knowledge. You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: You can increase one ability score by 2 and a different ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores of your choice by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Changelings mature slightly faster than humans but share a similar lifespan—typically a century or less. While a changeling can shapeshift to conceal their age, the effects of aging still catch up to them. They usually mature at 15, and live to around 90 or 100.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Many changelings tend to embrace good, while others embrace evil. Some err towards the path of abiding law, while others lean towards the freedom that chaos can bring. Often time a changeling can end up between a lot of these, but due to their nature and the experiences such a nature brings, it is exceedingly rare that one is truly neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Changelings are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall in their natural form. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Change Appearance
entries: As an action, you can change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight, but not so much that your size changes. You can make yourself appear as a member of another race, though none of your game statistics change. You can't duplicate the appearance of a creature you've never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. Your clothing and equipment aren't changed by this trait.
You stay in the new form until you use an action to revert to your true form or until you die. If you die, you revert to your true form over the course of 1 minute.

entries.name: Natural Deceiver
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill.

entries.name: Blessing of the Ambiguous One
entries: You always know when you are targeted by a spell of the Divination school. If you would be targeted by such a spell, as a reaction you can call upon the blessing of your ancestry. You are placed under the effects of the {@spell nondetection} spell, but with a duration of 1 hour instead of 8 hours. Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest. Activating this ability isn't perceptible by any means.

entries.name: Gift for Teaching
entries: You have a knack for imparting what you have learned onto others due to being able to mimic their bodies. Over the course of 1 hour, you can teach a creature proficiency with a skill you are proficient in. The creature must have an Intelligence of 6 or higher and be able to communicate with you. This proficiency lasts until the creature finishes a long rest, or until another creature benefits from the trait in this way. A creature can't benefit from this trait from more than one source, and other changelings can't benefit from this trait either.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Lagavir mature at the same rate as humans, and can live to be around 200 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The lagavir rarely align themselves with anything other than the interests of their heritage and home, but their cousins of the forest - the Durumir - often ground their beliefs in lawful neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Lagavir generally average over 7 feet tall in height, weighing in between 180 and 250 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Lagomorphic Legs
entries: You can long jump up to your walking speed and high jump up to half of your walking speed, with or without a running start, unless you would be able to jump longer or higher.

entries.name: Ancestral Glimpse
entries: When you finish a long rest, roll a {@dice d20} and record the number rolled, as a glimpse into an ancestor's experiences that you correct when chanced upon. If it is a 20, reroll it. The first time you roll that number on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it becomes a 20. The glimpse is lost once used, or when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: Life in the forests has lent you the language of the Fey, to commune with them when they arrive as your ancestors once did. You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: The lagavir fall into two categories, depending upon how they focus upon their heritage and view the path to peace; Ana-Vir, and Vir-Ana.

entries.name: Inherited Athleticism
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics} skill.

entries.name: Drop-Kick
entries: When a creature is in the path of your jump, you can attempt to shove them {@condition prone} as part of the movement, dropkicking them in the process. Regardless of the outcome, you end your jump in the closest unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target. Once you use this ability, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Inherited Eloquence
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill.

entries.name: Consultation
entries: When you would make an ability check that requires Intelligence or Wisdom, you can use Charisma instead. Once you do so, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Age
entries: Dajix mature at 15 years of age, and can live up to 100 years old, but their lack of boundaries to which they will extend their devotion and overall recklessness makes it rare for one to live beyond 60.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The dajix are unpredictable, irregular, and often go against anything that doesn't fit the narrative that best suits the object of their devotion. They are usually chaotic, and are very rarely lawful.

entries.name: Size
entries: Dajix are usually between 4 feet tall and 6 feet tall, rarely differing in weight from a healthy human of the same height. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Dajixian Culture
entries: You gain proficiency in {@skill Deception} and one type of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} of your choice.

entries.name: Obsession
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You're {@condition charmed} by that creature for 1 minute, or until it dies. While {@condition charmed} by that creature, you're immune to being {@condition charmed} by any other creature. You can't use this trait again until you finish a short or long rest. If you are immune to being {@condition charmed}, you ignore the immunity.

entries.name: Painful Love
entries: Being {@condition charmed} by a creature doesn't prevent you from attacking it or targeting it with harmful effects or abilities, and when you become {@condition charmed} by a creature, you have advantage on the next attack roll you make against it within the next minute.

entries.name: Pursue Love
entries: While a creature you're {@condition charmed} by is within line of sight, your movement speed increases by 15 feet, and you can't willingly move away from it.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly toward the gentler aspects of chaos. They value and protect others' freedom as well as their own, and they are more often good than not.

entries.name: Size
entries: Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Trance
entries: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. (The Common word for such meditation is "trance.") While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.
{@note If you meditate during a long rest, you finish the rest after only 4 hours. You otherwise obey all the rules for a long rest; only the duration is changed.}

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Elvish. Elvish is fluid, with subtle intonations and intricate grammar. Elven literature is rich and varied, and their songs and poems are famous among other races. Many bards learn their language so they can add Elvish ballads to their repertoires.

entries.name: Lunar Guidance
entries: You cannot have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks based on sight.

entries.name: Moonlight Walker
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill. Additionally, you can take the Hide action as a bonus action at night, and while the moon is full and the sky is visible, you don't need to be obscured to hide.

entries.name: Ethereality
entries: As a bonus action, you can vanish from your current plane of existence and appear in the Ethereal Plane until the start of your next turn, as per the {@spell blink} spell but with a duration of 1 round. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Star's Sight
entries: You have advantage on saving throws made to resist being blinded.

entries.name: Unending Breath
entries: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you're not incapacitated.

entries.name: Healing Energies
entries: As a bonus action, you can touch a creature and cause it to regain a number of hit points equal to your Charisma modifier. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
At 3rd level, you can instead cast the {@spell lesser restoration} spell using this feature.

entries.name: Solar Channelling
entries: You know the {@spell light} cantrip, and can choose to make the duration until dispelled. If you do so, the spell ends when you cast it again. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it. Additionally, when you create light through a spell you cast, you can choose to make the light as sunlight.

optionalfeature.name: Augmented Shadow
entries: The bestowed shadow's claw attack advances from a {@dice d4} to a {@dice d12}, and it gains proficiency in all saving throws.

optionalfeature.name: Cunning Shade
entries: The bestowed shadow can take the Dash, Disengage, or Help action as a bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Imbuement
entries: When you cast the {@spell eldritch blast} cantrip, you can forgo any number of beams to allow your bestowed shadow to make that many additional attacks with the Attack action on its next turn. So long as you forgo at least one beam, its reach extends to 10 feet, and its attacks are magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances and immunities until the start of your next turn..

optionalfeature.name: Vigorous Defender
entries: The bestowed shadow's hit point maximum equals six times your warlock level + your Charisma modifier + the bestowed shadow's Constitution modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Malleable Dark
entries: The bestowed shadow can change its size to Small, Medium, or Large as an action, immediately ending any other size-altering effects upon it. While Small, the bestowed shadow has advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, and can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than it. While Large, the bestowed shadow has advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks, and its carrying capacity (including maximum load and maximum lift) is doubled. While it is not Medium in this way, the bestowed shadow cannot be affected by the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell.

optionalfeature.name: The Swift Umbra
entries: The bestowed shadow's speed increases by 20 feet, its Dexterity score increases by 2, and its AC becomes 15 + its Dexterity modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Twilight Fiend
entries: The bestowed shadow's type becomes fiend, and the damage type of its attacks changes to cold, fire, or lightning, chosen when you finish a long rest. Additionally, it has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

creature.name: Bestowed Shadow

hp.special: equal to four times your level in this class + your Charisma modifier + the bestowed shadow's Constitution modifier

languages: speaks the languages you can speak

trait.name: Return to Darkness
entries: As a bonus action, the shadow can become a part of your shadow once more. You can use a bonus action on your turn to activate the shadow, and it appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of you. The shadow cannot manifest in nonmagical darkness, but if you are in magical darkness, it can manifest anywhere in the radius that you can see. Additionally, magical darkness doesn't impede the shadow's darkvision.

trait.name: Shadow of Yourself
entries: The bestowed shadow uses your proficiency bonus in place of its own. Additionally, it uses your Charisma score, has proficiency in Charisma saving throws if you do, and increases its other ability scores by an amount equal to your Charisma modifier.

trait.name: Without Form
entries: The shadow can only be damaged by attacks, unless targeted by an effect that targets no other creatures.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) slashing damage.

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: An ash-covered diary or journal, a set of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} (one of your choice), a straw hat, a holy symbol of a Good deity, a set of ash-covered {@item traveler's clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 5gp

entries.name: Requirement: Non-Evil Alignment
entries: To take this background, one must have dwelled within the ashes for long enough to have already been affected by them to some degree. As a result of this, you cannot take this background if you start as an Evil-aligned character.

entries.name: Feature: Spirit of Justice
entries: Wherever you go, people find themselves drawn to you for comfort. They see you as kind, and mark you as a Good and just soul upon first meeting you, regardless of how your actions have swayed your alignment on the axes. You might find yourself thrust into a position where your input can mean the difference between life or death of a prisoner, or seeking your advice on how they should move forward with a moral quandary.
You can use this to your advantage, as people are less likely to oppose your presence in discussions of importance, and often value your input after even the briefest of introductions.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Ash Dwellers hold some aspect of the Platinum Dragon's soul within themselves, and are usually soft-hearted, but can also be strong willed and determined. They usually prefer to see the good in someone's heart than to dismiss them as irredeemable.

rows: If I see someone in trouble, I can't help but want to solve their woes.
It takes a lot for any heat to be too much. In fact, much of the world is too cold for my liking.
A long walk for me usually spans over twenty-four hours. Anything shorter is brisk.
Having anything but hills of ash around me is unnerving. I'm not used to seeing buildings, trees, or anything dotting the horizon.
I cover my hands in fire ash to feel closer to home.
Even if I'm picky about the people I travel with, I always delight in having new personalities around me.
I can get a little overzealous when I'm invested in my objective. Just a little. I don't know why everyone thinks it's a problem.
I don't value wealth any more than I value the people I can help with it.
Being Good is nice and all, but a part of me does miss having darker desires.
I feel more ready to die for my cause than I do to live for it.
Justice. I am honor-bound to see justice through to its end, as the law dictates, and will suffer no transgressions against it. (Lawful)
Mercy. To suffer is to be mortal, but to stand by while others suffer and do nothing is unforgivable. (Good)
Reform. Not all laws are just. If my head tells me the law while my heart tells me that law is wrong, I must stand by them both and bring reform. (Chaotic)
Wrath. Eons of anger for the unjust death of the Platinum Dragon will be set upon the Mother's children, this I swear. (Evil)
Peace. Nothing is black and white. Good can become evil, law can become chaos. To stand apart will allow me to bring harmony between them. (Neutral)
Generosity. Wealth serves a greater purpose than merely to be hoarded. It should be given to the masses, so that all may benefit and live. (Good)
My family, clan, or tribe is the most important thing in my life, even when they are far from me.
The ashes that linger upon my clothes are of those I couldn't help see the errors of their ways, and their faces stay with me as long as their ashes do.
I am taking bettering myself as a personal challenge, as there are many inner vices to overcome.
The dragon that slew my family saw the error of their ways soon after, and I'm trying to find the will to forgive them.
My wealth goes to the orphanage I grew up in when possible.
I've fallen in love with a dragon.
I often give away too much money, and end up unable to feed myself or rent a room to sleep for the night.
When it comes to creatures bigger than me, I've learned better than to try and fight them.
I find it hard to punish someone or make them follow through on their debts.
I'm way too trusting, and unwilling to believe someone's playing me, even when all evidence points to it.
Whilst I wear a loving and kind exterior, I'm still as rotten inside as I was when I first entered the Ashes.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: {@skill Arcana}, plus one from among {@skill History}, {@skill Nature}, and {@skill Religion}

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A bottle of black ink, a quill, 10 sheets of paper, a letter you can't get rid of explaining your exile and the reason behind it, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp

entries.name: Prohibition: Wizardry
entries: In a situation as extreme as exile, the possibility that you discovered the greatest secrets of the Spires couldn't be ignored, and as a result you have been prohibited from practicing Wizardry, where it seems those secrets are supposedly most dangerous in practice.

entries.name: Feature: Arcane Specialist
entries: From your time working in the Spire, you've become sensitive to even the faintest arcane signatures around you. As a result of this, by focusing you can usually tell what school of magic an item holds, a person has recently been affected by, or a ritual circle emulates without magic. Your DM might state that something was performed in a way so archaic that you can't make heads or tails of it, or that you might require specialized equipment that only the Spire has in order to discover the truth behind the target of your curiosity.

entries.name: Department
entries: To determine which department you were a part of, roll a {@dice d12} or choose from the options in the table below.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Exiles often hold grudges against the Spire for the nature of their dismissal, but sometimes they couldn't care less. Their characteristics are often reflective of someone who is aloof, but they can also realise that just as they might have been, judging others unfairly without giving them a chance can just lead to bitterness and lost opportunities.

rows: As a victim of cynicism myself, I refuse to subject others to it.
The presence of authority is discomforting to me. I'd much rather be the one in charge.
I still harbor respect for the Spire and those who work there, and can't stand people who look down on it.
I find myself drawn to those who are outcast. Nobody deserves to be alone.
Everything must be a part of something greater, otherwise why would it happen?
If there's one thing I learned from my mentor, it's that rambling about something eventually gets your point across.
I try my hardest to be straight-forward and communicative.
People often think I don't listen, due to how despondent I can be.
Preservation. All knowledge must be preserved for future generations. (Neutral)
Secrecy. Some knowledge must be kept secret for the good of all. (Lawful)
Honesty. All knowledge must be shared, so that all can make their own educated decisions. (Chaotic)
Altruism. Knowledge is only useful if it can benefit the many. The ultimate goal of learning is using that information to make the world a better place. (Good)
Exploitation. Those less knowledgeable than myself can be used for my endeavors. (Evil)
Pursuit. The acquirement of knowledge is the only thing that matters in life. Comfort, happiness, and even my own well-being are secondary. (Neutral)
I was exiled for something out of my control, and I must find the one responsible.
Upon being exiled, I have become obsessed with proving my worth, and showing the world just how wrong they were.
I heard whisperings of a dark rumor I am too terrified to repeat, and now shudder at the dark, unknown recesses of the world.
I've been searching my whole life for the answer to a certain question.
Though we were both exiled, my mentor remained in the Spire. I'll do anything to find out why.
My reason for exile is something that causes me immense guilt, and whether intentionally or subconsciously, I seek to atone in everything I do.
I find it hard to get along with people when I meet them for the first time, due to my bitterness.
It's a bad habit of mine to not inform people of vital information, since I'm used to keeping quiet.
I obey the law, even if it would means someone stays dead that could have been saved.
Seeing someone do something wrong that I can do better infuriates me, and I can't keep quiet.
I overlook obvious solutions in favor of complicated ones.
I'm drawn to forbidden knowledge like a moth to a flame.

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A {@item riding saddle|phb} that is worn but well-maintained, a set of {@item leatherworker's tools|phb}, {@item saddlebags|phb}, a reminder of your first mount (such as a leather patch, a scale), a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 20 sp

entries.name: Feature: Eye for Talent
entries: You've seen a wide variety of mounts from all walks of life. You've bonded with them, rode with them, cared for them, and you can immediately tell where a mount might be deficient or efficient. If you spend at least 10 minutes looking over a creature to gauge its capabilities as a mount, you can determine anything extraordinary about it; a drake with harder scales but a weak heart, a pony with an odd gait but unyielding bravery, and qualities of that nature. You can use this information to gauge the true value of the creature, by both its current prowess and its potential.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: A Rider's view on nature and the inherent value of wildlife has been shaped by their experiences. Perhaps they believe that bonds are to be treasured, or that due to the constant coming and going of companions, they're don't want to make lasting friendships. Sometimes they think animals are so much more capable than we give them credit for, while others feel that they're good for little more than meat and transport.

rows: At a gathering, I'm more likely to sit with the animals than to talk with anyone of importance.
I find walking anywhere on foot to be tedious.
There are few things better to me in life than feeling the wind rushing past me.
When I'm mounted, my bravery is unmatched. When I'm dismounted, I'm...less brave, to put it lightly.
I'm rather quick to trust and form bonds, to make up for those I have lost along the way.
Little can sour my optimistic attitude, I always try to see the best in a situation.
I've spent so long talking with creatures that aren't sentient that I have a bad habit of forgetting how to talk to people.
I hate to see anything suffer.
Redemption. All life deserves a second chance. (Good)
Education. Those less knowledgeable should be taught and educated as we have been, where possible. (Lawful)
Liberty. Nothing should be chained or confined; all should be free to live and die as they please. (Lawful)
Ambition. To have a great partner is to have a great goal, and mine will be greater than all. (Any)
Supremacy. Those less intelligent than us are meant to be subservient to us. (Evil)
Wild. The wilderness is more important than all the constructs of civilization. (Chaotic)
I once found a spectacularly talented creature in the wild, and I've searched for it ever since.
My first companion was poached, and I'll never love another like it.
I've been through a portal to another realm, and I want to find it again to search for talent there.
I'll die for those who'll die for me.
The love of an animal is the purest love there is.
If it came down to life or death, I would sacrifice myself for the one who wasn't able to save themselves, as someone once did for me.
I am deeply impulsive, and often follow my whims even when it's inappropriate.
The death of an animal can leave me inconsolable.
Without a companion by my side, I'm easily terrified.
I will not stand anyone that calls a companion a 'pet'.
I can't let go of a creature I can't keep.
I despise poachers. They deserve nothing short of death, and I will hunt them down until achieved.

spell.name: Killing Joke

components.m: a blood soaked feather that is waved in the air

entries: A creature of your choice that you can see and that can hear you within range suddenly curl up their mouth into a wide smile, uncontrollably laughing.

The target must succeed a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature falls under the effects of the spell and is {@condition incapacitated}, falling {@condition prone} in a fit of laughter. At the end of each of the creature's turns, it must repeat the saving throw. On a failure, the creature remains {@condition incapacitated} or becomes {@condition incapacitated} if it had recovered prior. On a success, the creature is no longer {@condition incapacitated}, but is still laughing as the spell persists. After failing three of these saving throws after the first, the creature begins to suffocate for the duration (see the rules for Suffocation), the creature is {@condition incapacitated} and has a speed of 0, and the creature stops making these saves.
