After succeeding on three of these saving throws, the creature recovers from the effects of the spell, and the spell ends for it.
Creature who do not need to breathe do not suffer the Suffocation effects.

spell.name: Finale
entries: The time has come for your show to enter its final act. You cast this spell, accepting your end, and go out with a bang.

You can cast any number of spells you know of 8th level or lower up to a total of 18 levels of spells, so long as they have a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action. You cannot cast the same spell twice, and you must provide the materials as the spell requires them, but you do not require the spell slots available to cast them.

After the effects of this spell transpire, you explode into confetti and die. This death cannot be prevented by any means, and you cannot be brought to life by any means short of the {@spell true resurrection} or {@spell wish} spell.

spell.name: Aftercare
entries: This spell is often used by healers working in areas of conflict and strife. You can perform one of the following effects on a target within range:

items: You cleanse all visible wounds on the target.
You cause a needle and thread within range to suture a wound closed with care and precision for 1 minute.
You cause any of the target's broken or fractured bones to slowly set in place for 1 minute, causing them as little pain and discomfort as possible.

You cause a roll of bandages within range to unfurl and begin wrapping around the target's wounds for 1 minute where you desire, or until you end the effect. When you end the effect, the bandages secure themselves as you wish, and then sever from the roll.

entries: You can also cast the spell with a casting time of 10 minutes, targeting a creature that is stable and at 0 hit points. The target must be within range of the spell for the duration of the casting time. When you finish casting the spell, the target regains 1 hit point.

spell.name: Crashing Wave
entries: The ocean's waves bend to your will, surrounding your weaponry with the force of the waves and crashing down.

The next time you hit a creature with a weapon attack before this spell ends, the weapon is covered in a wave of water as you strike, dealing an extra {@dice 2d6} bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 30 feet away from you.

spell.name: Swim Through Space
entries: Neither ground or air, sand or earth, or anything else can impede you. Until the end of your turn, you gain a fly speed and a burrow speed of 60 feet.

spell.name: Arrow Catcher
entries: You create a shield visible to you and your allies that hovers idly over the target's head until the spell ends. While this shield is active, nonmagical ranged attacks against the target have disadvantage.

spell.name: Peaceful Walk
entries: You extend an enchanting aura of calm in a 30-foot radius sphere centered on yourself, forcing everyone to walk with measured steps. A creature other than yourself that starts its turn in the sphere must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have its speed halved until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can choose one creature to not be affected for each slot level above 1st.

spell.name: Weapon of Choice
entries: You imbue a weapon you're holding with a specific signature, empowering it. Until the spell ends, you are considered proficient with the weapon, and you gain a +1 bonus to weapon damage rolls made with it. The spell ends early if you let go of the weapon.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the bonus increases to +2. When you use a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the bonus increases to +3.

spell.name: Dusk Pulse
entries: An inky blackness ripples upon the ground. All hostile creatures within a 30-foot radius centred on you must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take {@dice 2d8} necrotic damage. A creature that succeeds takes half as much damage. A creature that failed the saving throw takes damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of their next turn, and sees as though in darkness. A creature that is {@condition blinded} in this way treats all terrain within the area as difficult terrain.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, both the initial and secondary instances of damage increase by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Old Town Road
entries: Every creature other than you within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be teleported to the space where they began their previous turn. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the range of the spell increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Scholar's Passion
entries: This spell is used by scholars and scribes to assist them in their work and to care for their books. You create one of the following effects:

items: This spell creates a perfect duplicate of any written or drawn document that you touch onto blank pages you supply.
You can copy up to 20 pages of text with one casting of this spell. Magical writing, including spellbook pages, can’t be copied with this spell.

This spell copies and translates any text that you touch onto blank pages you supply. You can copy up to 20 pages of text with one casting of this spell. This spell can copy the text into any language the caster is capable of understanding.

With this spell you can touch a book or scroll and absorb the knowledge contained within as if you had just read it. This is equivalent to a solid reading but not deep study — the caster does not gain perfect recall of the information, just whatever they would have gotten from reading it completely once. If you can't read the language of the source, the spell has no effect. This spell cannot be used to prepare spells or to cast magical scrolls, nor does it have any effect when reading a magical book (such as a tome of understanding).

This spell will completely repair and clean a mundane book or scroll given that greater than half of the original is still available. It will even restore missing or faded pages. The spell has no effect on a magic book, spell scrolls or spell books.
This spell will animate a book giving it a mouth and voice to read its pages aloud. The book will speak until finished or told to be quiet.

spell.name: Cold Snap
entries: A chilly frost saps the heat from your foes around a point you choose within range. Each creature within a 5-foot-radius sphere of that point must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@dice 6d6} cold damage and has their movement speed halved until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage, and their movement speed isn't halved.
If a creature took fire damage since the end of your last turn, they take an additional {@dice 3d6} cold damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Deny Form

time.condition: which you can take when a creature begins to change its shape through a spell or effect

entries: You let forth a refutation at a shapechanging target you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@dice 6d6} force damage, its form is reverted, and it cannot change shape again until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Lightning Spear
entries: A bolt of lightning forms in your hand, as you throw it towards your quarry. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@dice 5d10} lightning damage and is knocked {@condition prone} or pushed 20 feet back (your choice).

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Rescue

time.condition: which you take when you see an ally within 120 feet of you drop to 0 hit points

entries: You teleport a willing or {@condition unconscious} creature you can see within range to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you, potentially saving them from dire circumstances. If the creature is dying, they are also stabilized.

spell.name: Shackles of Pain
entries: You send forth ethereal spiked chains to shackle another, making them destined to share your plight. Choose one creature you can see within range to make a Wisdom saving throw. If it fails, the target is linked to you for the duration. While linked in this way, whenever your hit points are reduced, the target's hit points are reduced by the same amount. The target can repeat the saving throw each time it loses hit points in this way, severing the link on a success.
These shackles remain while the target is within 1 mile of you, even if it is in the Ethereal Plane.

spell.name: Army of the Dead

components.m: a black onyx stone worth at least 2,000 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: And so came the day that the dead would walk the earth, under your command, if only briefly. You mutter an unholy incantation that crushes the black onyx stone in your hand and scatters its dust in a 300-foot radius around you.

You can cast this spell only at night. You can create 200 hit points worth of undead at points you choose within range. These undead can be {@creature ghoul||ghouls}, {@creature ghast||ghasts}, {@creature mummy||mummies}, {@creature skeleton||skeletons}, {@creature wight||wights}, or {@creature zombie||zombies}. Each undead has average hit points, and it uses your proficiency bonus in place of its own. If you can see a corpse within range, you can create an undead from the corpse, reducing the number of hit points needed by 10 for that undead. You can't have more undead than your level.

As a bonus action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 120 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the creature only defends itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.
The creatures are under your control until the spell ends, after which they become inanimate once more.

spell.name: Virulent Scourge
entries: You weave together a serpentine whip that uncoils from your hand, slowly weaving in place in wait for your command. This magical whip lasts until the spell ends, and its properties are the same as a whip, except you make melee spell attacks with it, and it deals poison damage equal to {@dice 2d12} + your spellcasting ability modifier on a hit. Additionally, once per turn you can cause the target to make a Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of their next turn.
If you drop the weapon, it dissipates at the end of the turn. Thereafter, while the spell persists, you can use a bonus action to cause the whip to reappear in your hand.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a 4th- or 5th-level spell slot, the damage increases to {@dice 3d12}. When you cast it using a 6th- or 7th- level spell slot, the damage increases to {@dice 4d12}. When you cast it using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the damage increases to {@dice 5d12}.

spell.name: Bulwark

time.condition: which you take when you or an ally within 5 feet of you are hit by an attack or targeted by the {@spell magic missile} spell

entries: A large shield of magical force appears and protects you and your allies. Until the start of your next turn, you and up to two allies of your choice within 5 feet of you have a +6 bonus to AC - including against the triggering attack - a +2 bonus to Dexterity saving throws, and you take no damage from the magic missile spell. If an ally benefiting from this spell moves 5 feet away from you, you can choose a new ally (no action required by you). This does not stack with the {@spell shield} spell.

spell.name: Healing Mist
entries: A 15-foot radius sphere of soft golden mist appears centered on a point of your choice within range. A creature within the sphere regains {@dice 1d4} hit points at the start of each of its turns. The sphere lasts for the duration or until a wind of moderate or greater speed (at least 10 miles per hour) disperses it.

spell.name: Dream Eater
entries: When you cast this spell, choose one creature within range. If that creature is conscious, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall {@condition unconscious}, and its hit point maximum is reduced by {@dice 1d10}. You regain hit points equal to the result. If the creature is {@condition unconscious}, there is no saving throw. While this spell is active, as a bonus action on each of your turns you can reduce the target's hit point maximum by {@dice 1d10} and heal yourself for the result. This spell automatically ends if the creature takes damage from a source other than this spell.

spell.name: Hammer Crush

time.condition: which you take when a creature provokes an opportunity attack

entries: You retaliate to the movement of someone attempting to leave your reach with a crushing blow. As part of the reaction used to cast this spell, you must make a melee attack with a bludgeoning weapon against the creature that provoked the opportunity attack within the spell's range, otherwise the spell fails. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects, takes an additional {@dice 6d8} bludgeoning damage, is knocked {@condition prone}, and has their movement speed reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

spell.name: Rancid Expulsion

components.m: a vial of acid worth at least 25gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You empty the contents of the vial into your mouth, the magic multiplying its volume explosively until it is expelled in a 60-foot cone of your choice. All creatures within the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 15d4} acid damage. A creature takes half as much damage on a success. For the duration, nonmagical objects and structures within the area are melted by the acid, and creatures that end their turn in the acid, or enter the area for the first time on a turn, take {@dice 4d4} acid damage. If damage from this spell leaves a target with 0 hit points, the target's body is dissolved.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the initial damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each slot level above 6th.

spell.name: Melthia's Spontaneous Arrival
entries: You teleport yourself and all willing creatures of your choice within range to the upper atmosphere of the plane you currently reside on.

spell.name: Terra Guardian
entries: The ground quakes, erupting spikes in a protective 30-foot radius, 15-foot high cylinder around you. All hostile creatures must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 6d4} piercing damage and be pushed beyond the radius. The erupted earth forms 15-foot tall obstacles at the end of the radius in the form of stalagmites, curved teeth akin to an earthen maw, or sharp boulders. Each 5-foot square of obstacles have an AC of 15, and 30 hit points.
While this spell is active, you can repeat the action on yourturn to change the radius and force hostile creatures to repeat the saving throw. Doing so restores the hit points of each obstacle.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the initial damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each slot level above 6th.

spell.name: Execution Block
entries: A colossal guillotine falls upon a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 12d10} slashing damage. If the creature is {@condition prone}, they make this saving throw with disadvantage. If this leaves the target with 0 hit points, you can cut off or destroy one of the target's heads if possible.

spell.name: Thunder Reign
entries: A vibration hums around hostile creatures you can see within 30 feet of you. Hostile creatures must succeed on a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the vibration grows in pitch for that creature for the duration, and if a target willingly moves (except to stand from {@condition prone}), it immediately takes {@dice 5d8} thunder damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. A creature can only take damage from this spell once per turn. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the initial damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 7th.

spell.name: Devouring Maw
entries: You call forth a calamitous serpent's likeness to a point you can see within range, causing a humongous maw to snap shut over it in a 100-foot radius sphere.

Creatures within the radius must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 8d10} force damage. A creature takes half as much damage on a success, and an object or structure takes the maximum damage. A creature is consumed if the damage from failing the saving throw leaves it with 0 hit points, as is a structure or object.

A consumed creature and everything it is wearing or carrying, except magic items, are consumed entirely, leaving behind no trace of its existence. The creature can be restored to life only by means of a true resurrection or a wish spell.

spell.name: Inferno Aura
entries: Wild flames of a vast inferno swirl erratically around you in a 30-foot radius. For the duration, all hostile creatures within range lose resistance to fire damage, and any time damage dice would be rolled to deal fire damage to a hostile creature, it takes maximum damage, instead of rolling.

spell.name: End of the World

components.m: everything you're carrying aside from your clothes

entries: Light, life, love, all are reduced to nothing. A void appears in a point you can see, and each creature in a 120-foot radius sphere centered on that point is teleported to a point of your choice within 15 feet of the center of the sphere. All creatures of CR 5 or lower that are teleported are instantly slain, and do not occupy a space.

Afterward, each creature in a 30-foot radius sphere centered on that point take {@dice 20d10} force damage, and are blinded and have their movement speed reduced to 0 until the end of their next turn.

spell.name: Grim Harvest
entries: The scythe of the reaper appears behind you, prepared to sacrifice your life to harm your foe.

You take {@dice 15d8} necrotic damage, and one creature of your choice that you can see within range takes damage equal to double the damage you took. You can choose to let the damage you take overcome any damage resistances or immunities you might have.

spell.name: Toward Earth
entries: You touch a creature. The first time the target takes falling damage, it gains immunity to the damage, and the spell ends.

spell.name: Lightning Helix
entries: You launch a twisted stroke of lightning at a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 2d8} lightning damage and make the saving throw again. If the target fails the Dexterity saving throw three times, the effect ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Tombstone
entries: You conjure a tombstone above a creature you can see within range, which crashes down on them. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@dice 4d6} bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful save, the target takes half damage, but suffers no other effect.
If you target an undead creature, it makes the saving throw with disadvantage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Bird's Eye
entries: You create an invisible, magical eye within range that hovers in the air above you and moves with you for the duration.
You mentally receive visual information from the eye, which has normal vision and can look in every direction.

As an action, you can change the eye's altitude by up to 120 feet in either direction. A solid barrier blocks the eye's movement, and if you're underground or indoors, the eye moves to within 5 feet of you and closes until you aren't.

spell.name: Caustic Swamp
entries: A swamp covers the ground in a 20-foot radius sphere centered on a point within range and turns it into difficult terrain. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes {@dice 4d4} acid damage. If this damage leaves a target with 0 hit points, the target's body is dissolved. Any organic object in the swamp that isn't being worn or carried, such as a corpse or food, is also dissolved.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the radius of the swamp increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Edge of Winter
entries: You create a massive blade made of frost and swing it in a 15-foot cone up to two times. Each time you swing the blade, you make a melee spell attack against each creature of your choice in the area, dealing {@dice 3d8} cold damage on a hit.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can swing the blade an additional time for every two slot levels above 4th.

spell.name: Lightning Vortex
entries: You launch a wicked stroke of lightning at a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 5d8} lightning damage and make the saving throw again with disadvantage. If the target fails the Dexterity saving throw three times, the effect ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Staff of Death
entries: You summon a staff with the appearance of a human spine. You can use the staff as a spellcasting focus. While holding the staff, you can use your action to hurl three skull shaped motes of darkness at targets within 120 feet of you. You can hurl them at one target or several. Make a ranged spell attack for each mote. On a hit, a target takes {@dice 2d10} necrotic damage. When you cast a spell on your turn while holding the staff, you can use your bonus action to hurl a mote of darkness.

spell.name: Toward Heaven

components.m: snow from the peak of a mountain

entries: A circular, horizontal plane of force springs into existence on the ground around you, centered on you. The plane is 15 feet in diameter and 1 inch thick. As an action while you're on the plane, you can cause the plane to move up to 120 feet in a straight line, causing anything on it to move with it. The plane can hold up to 5,000 pounds. If more weight is placed on the plane, the spell ends, and everything on it falls to the ground.

spell.name: Holy Rain

components.m: holy water worth at least 25gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You cause holy water to rain around you in a 60-foot radius. The rain moves with you, centered on you. When you cast this spell, you can choose any number of creatures you can see. When a chosen creature ends its turn within the rain, it regains hit points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier. When a fiend or undead ends its turn within the rain, it takes radiant damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

subclass.name: Mad Dog
subclassFeatures: Way of the Mad Dog|Monk||Mad Dog|TheDarkAscension|3

subclass.name: Branch Reader
subclassFeatures: Path of the Branch Reader|Barbarian||Branch Reader|TheDarkAscension|3
Knowledge of the Great Tree|Barbarian||Branch Reader|TheDarkAscension|14

subclassFeature.name: College of Villainy
entries: Bards of the College of Villainy are monstrous souls, taking joy in whatever insults and misery they can force unto their foes. Wearing a smile in the face of danger and a wicked grin in the face of certain death, they can often be found within dark alleys, outlined as a scarlet shade wielding a blade dripping with crimson ichor. Other times these villains are the victims, laying beaten upon the ground with harmonies of dreadful joy echoing from their hoarse throats. These twisted performers turn infamy into an art form; after all, no matter how many times a villain might die, they are never forgotten.

entries.name: Villainous Guile
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you've learned to become a true nuisance to your enemies. You gain proficiency with martial weapons. Additionally, while you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Insult the Injury
entries: Also at 3rd level, you've learned to laugh in the face of pain, wounding your enemies' psyche. When you take damage from a hostile creature you can see within 10 feet of you that can hear you, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration as a reaction. The target takes psychic damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1), and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn.
At 5th, 10th, and 15th level in this class, this damage increases by an additional {@dice 1d6}.

subclassFeature.name: Twist the Knife
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can cut through to a creature's mind with your weapon. You learn the {@spell vicious mockery} cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you learn a different {@filter bard cantrip|spells|class=bard|level=0|source=phb} of your choice. This cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.

When you hit a creature with a weapon attack as part of the Attack action, you can use your bonus action to cast {@spell vicious mockery}, adding your Charisma modifier to the damage roll. On a success, the target takes half as much damage instead.

subclassFeature.name: Petty Inspiration
entries: Starting at 14th level, your laugh is so potent and loud that such pettiness inspires your allies. The range of your Insult the Injury feature increases to 60 feet. Additionally, when you deal damage to a creature using Insult the Injury, you can immediately grant a Bardic Inspiration die to an ally you can see without expending a use of the feature. The ally can only use the die on rolls against or caused by the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Armiger
entries: Those who earn the right to call themselves an Armiger are few and far between. Within their hands is a magic passed down from person to person, rumored to have been created by a lost line of royalty. Compared to an Eldritch Knight and their spellcasting, an Armiger uses their magic for the sole purpose of wielding their vast and varied array of weaponry.

entries.name: Arcane Armory
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your weaponry lies at your beck and call. As an action, you can add a melee weapon that you're holding to your Arcane Armory. You can have a number of weapons in your Arcane Armory up to twice your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 2). Your Arcane Armory can't contain two of the same type of weapon, such as two rapiers. You can remove a weapon from your Arcane Armory at any time. While in your Arcane Armory, a weapon gains the following benefits.

items: As a bonus action, you can cause any number of weapons in your Arcane Armory to vanish.

As a bonus action, you can cause any number of weapons you caused to vanish to appear and float around you. You can draw a floating weapon without an object interaction, and if you drop it within 5 feet of you, it floats once more. A creature can't take a floating weapon unless you allow it.

You can make a ranged weapon attack as if the weapon had the Thrown (range 20/60) property. If the weapon already has the Thrown property, attacking at long range with it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack roll.

entries.name: Warp Strike
entries: At 3rd level, you call your weaponry to yourself and vice versa. When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using a weapon in your Arcane Armory, you can teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of that creature. If you do so, you can retrieve the weapon and immediately make a melee weapon attack using the weapon against the creature you hit. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Heraldic Weaponry
entries: Starting at 7th level, weaponry you craft can't be mistaken. You gain proficiency in smith's tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses them.
You can craft a non-magical melee weapon within one day, regardless of the weapon's market value, and you can leave a signature on the weapon which can't be perfectly forged.
Additionally, the weapons in your Arcane Armory count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

subclassFeature.name: Flexible Fighting Style
entries: Starting at 10th level, you aren't held back by a single style. Once on each of your turns, you can replace the option you chose for your Fighting Style feature with another option.

subclassFeature.name: Army of One
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you hit a creature with three weapon attacks using three different types of weapon on your turn, you deal extra force damage equal to your Intelligence modifier x 3 (minimum 3). For example, you deal extra damage if you hit with a rapier, shortsword, and dagger.

subclassFeature.name: Royal Armory
entries: Starting at 18th level, you access the power of every Armiger before you, back to the time when it was wielded by royalty. As a bonus action, you can call forth the power of kings. For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your movement speed is doubled, and your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.
When you hit with a weapon attack using a weapon in your Arcane Armory, you deal maximum damage with the weapon's damage die, instead of rolling.
You can use your Warp Strike without expending a use.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Curse Bearer
entries: The foolish who decide to become a Curse Bearer tend to live a short life, their body, mind, and soul wracked by torment in exchange for power: Their decision cannot be called correct. Whether their aim is to protect, to avenge, or to die an early death, none aside from the Curse Bearer themselves know.

entries.name: Cursed Covenant
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you call countless curses upon yourself for the sake of strength. As a bonus action, you can invoke a number of curses. The curses last for 1 minute, or until you end the curses as an action. You can't invoke curses again until the curses end. For each curse invoked, you take {@dice 1d6} necrotic damage, which ignores resistance and immunity.
The effect of the curses depend on the curses you invoke from the options below.

entries.name: Luxuria
entries: You are immune to being {@condition charmed}.

entries.name: Gula
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.

entries.name: Avaritia
entries: You can remove disadvantage on your weapon attack rolls, but you can't gain advantage on the roll.

entries.name: Acedia
entries: Your speed increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Ira
entries: You are immune to being {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Invidia
entries: You can remove advantage on weapon attack rolls against you, but disadvantage can't be imposed on the roll.
You can invoke two of these curses at once. You can invoke two additional curses at 7th and 10th level.

subclassFeature.name: Last Fragment
entries: Starting at 7th level, you keep a fragment of yourself safe from harm. When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can end any invoked curses and drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature if you don't have any curses invoked, and once you use this feature, you can't invoke curses again for 1 minute.
Additionally, you have advantage on saving throws against curses and can break attunement to cursed items normally.

subclassFeature.name: Meaningless Pride
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can invoke the cruelest curse. If you invoke six curses at once, you can invoke the curse below as part of the same bonus action.

entries.name: Superbia
entries: At the end of each of your turns, you can inflict 7 necrotic damage to a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, which ignores resistance and immunity.

subclassFeature.name: End of the Night
entries: Starting at 15th level, you've lived longer than you should've. If you die except by old age, you return to life with 1 hit point and with any mortal wounds closed in {@dice 1d4} hours, as long as you aren't missing any body parts integral for your survival.
Additionally, you are immune to curses unless you willingly choose to be cursed, including the effects of cursed items.

subclassFeature.name: All the World's Evil
entries: At 18th level, you're closer to a curse than a person. When you're damaged by a creature you can see within 30 feet of you while you have seven curses invoked, you can inflict the same amount of damage as necrotic damage to the creature as a reaction, which ignores resistance and immunity. This damage can be no higher than your fighter level.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Judgement
entries: The Oath of Judgement is made by a paladin with the belief that any crime can be forgiven, but a few of them can only be forgiven by death. Whether they're called an executioner, an inquisitor, or insultingly a butcher, they work within the law and allow the indicted to atone for whatever deed they've done. To imprison a thief or execute a murderer, only the appropriate punishment for the crime committed can be called judgement. To act otherwise is merely vengeance.

entries.name: Tenets of Judgement
entries: The tenets of the Oath of Judgement are nearly identical regardless of where they're sworn or who they're sworn by. As they abide by the law and are empowered by it, they tend to be lawful in alignment, though whether they're good or evil is dependent on the paladin. Their tenets can't be mistaken.

entries.name: Heed the Judge
entries: I will heed the verdict passed down on the guilty by the law, and I will not stray from that verdict.

entries.name: Heed the Jury
entries: The law is made by society and not by an individual, I will not decide the law by my will alone.

entries.name: Be the Executioner
entries: I will punish the guilty as they deserve and allow them to be forgiven for their crime.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Be Just
entries: As an action, choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition prone} and have a speed of 0 until the end of its next turn. A creature that has directly killed a humanoid within the last year has disadvantage on the saving throw.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Be Dead
entries: As an action, make a melee weapon attack. If the target is {@condition incapacitated}, {@condition prone}, or {@condition restrained}, the attack deals maximum damage on a hit.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Judgement
entries: Starting at 7th level, the guilty cannot raise their head in your presence. While you're not {@condition incapacitated}, an aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through total cover. If a hostile creature is {@condition prone} and in the aura, it costs the creature an amount of movement equal to its movement speed to stand.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Hope for Tomorrow
entries: Starting at 15th level, the guilty can have hope for tomorrow, they can be forgiven once they've received their punishment. When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with an attack or spell, you can choose to knock the creature out instead.

Additionally, you deny hope to those who don't deserve it, only death can redeem them now. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can attempt to execute it. After the attack, the target is reduced to 0 hit points if it has hit points equal to your paladin level or fewer. If it dies, you can cut off or destroy one of the target's heads if possible.

subclassFeature.name: Holy Order
entries: Starting at 20th level, you can become an avatar of absolute judgement. Using your action, you undergo a transformation.
For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: You can use Be Just as a bonus action, and using Be Just or Be Dead doesn't expend a use of Channel Divinity.
Your movement speed is doubled, and your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.
If you attempt to execute a creature, it dies if the attack reduces its hit points to equal to or less than twice your paladin level.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Clemency
entries: The Oath of Clemency is one taken by a paladin who often have seen too much conflict, strife, and dissent in their life, and seek a resolution for it all. They are often referred to as peacekeepers, but are at other times revered and welcomed as healers. A paladin that has taken this oath seek to resolve conflict wherever they can find it, to mend the wounds inflicted upon body and soul, to repair the bonds broken and severed between former allies, and to create new bonds where previously existed only animosity.

Many of these paladins find themselves alongside angels - those seen as perfect embodiments of all that is good - to bring peace, while many might find themselves facing angels, for often the perfect servants of good find themselves unable to stand the taint of evil in their existence. Such is the way of seeking clemency; it cannot truly exist for one if it does not exist for all.

entries.name: Tenets of Clemency
entries: The tenets of the Oath of Clemency differ from one to another based on their perception of peace and the means that must be used to bring it, but invariably, the tenets will include or emphasize the following tenets.

entries.name: Peace for All
entries: When tensions are high and unnecessary conflict arises, you must bring peace between them, be it through words or action.

entries.name: Mercy for All
entries: When conflict arises, necessary or not, you must show mercy to your foes and understand that like you, they too fight for clemency in these times.

entries.name: Health for All
entries: When the wounds of conflict cut deepest, you must give of yourself and dip into your deepest reserves to cleanse any who need it. Friend or foe, an open wound is one that must be mended.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

entries.caption: Oath of Clemency Spells
rows: {@spell protection from evil and good}, {@spell sanctuary}

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Pacify
entries: As an action, choose any number of creatures you can see within 30 feet of you. A creature targeted by this effect must make a Wisdom saving throw. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw. A creature at 0 hit points automatically fails this saving throw, and if it is dying, it is stabilized. On a failure, the creature can't attack, cause another creature to make a saving throw, or deal damage to another creature for 24 hours. Until the effect ends, any creature who targets a creature under this effect with an attack or a harmful effect must first make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target or lose the attack or effect.

The effect ends on a creature if it is harmed by an attack or effect, or if it is targeted with an attack or harmful effect by you or your allies. If a creature targeted by Pacify succeeds its saving throw or the effect ends for it, it can't be affected by Pacify again for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Replenishment
entries: As a bonus action, you can focus your sacred energies to replenish your pool of healing power. You regain a number of points in your Lay on Hands pool equal to your paladin level + your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Clemency
entries: Starting at 7th level, those within your vicinity are subject to your peaceful will. While you're not {@condition incapacitated}, an aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through total cover. When you use your action to use Lay on Hands, you can target any number of creatures as part of that action, and can target any creature within the aura. You decide how many points to spend on each individual creature you chose.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit of Clemency
entries: Starting at 15th level, your desire to resolve conflict and save as many lives as possible empowers your restorative powers. When you cast a paladin spell using a spell slot of 1st level or higher that restores hit points to a creature, you can choose to place the creature under the effects of the {@spell protection from evil and good} spell, or the {@spell sanctuary} spell, until the end of your next turn. If the spell restores hit points to more than one creature, the effect you choose applies to all targets.
Additionally, when you restore hit points to a creature at 0 hit points, the creature regains additional hit points equal to your paladin level.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of Peace
entries: At 20th level, as an action, you become a beacon for peace. For the next minute, blessed rain pours down in a 30-foot radius around you. You gain the following benefits:

items: Creatures of your choice that end their turn in the blessed rain regain a number of hit points equal to your Charisma modifier. Fiends and undead instead take radiant damage equal to this amount.

While you are conscious, if a creature within the rain would die from a spell or effect that kills them when reduced to 0 hit points, such as the {@spell disintegrate} spell or a {@creature nightwalker|MTF|nightwalker's} Life Eater trait, the killing effect is nullified. Creatures reduced to 0 hit points in the rain are automatically stabilized, and you can cast the rescue spell as a reaction on them without expending a spell slot.
You can use Pacify as a bonus action, and using Pacify doesn't expend a use of Channel Divinity.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: The Night Serpent
entries: Dendar, Nidhogg, World Eater, Mother of the Night Parade. Regardless of her name, the Night Serpent has existed since the dawn of time to feed on the dreams and nightmares of mortals and deities alike. When she has eaten enough of them to satisfy herself, the world will fall into her maw.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Night Serpent lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Spiral Serpent
entries: Starting at 1st level, you have a shadow serpent coiled around each of your arms. You can use Charisma instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes and any Strength (Athletics) check involving a grapple. If you have a free hand, your unarmed strikes made with it are magical and have a reach of 15 feet as the serpent strikes.

Additionally, if you have a free hand, you can use your bonus action to make an unarmed strike with it. If you hit, the takes extra necrotic damage equal to your warlock level and is knocked {@condition prone}. Once you use this bonus action, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Creeping Darkness
entries: Starting at 6th level, the shadow serpent can leave your arm and slither through the dark. As an action, you can uncoil a shadow serpent from your arm in a space within 5 feet of you. You can't use your Spiral Serpent feature with the hand on that arm while the serpent is uncoiled, and you can only uncoil one serpent each arm. You can see as if you were in the space of the serpent, and can move the serpent up to 30 feet as a bonus action. When seeing through the space of the serpent, you have darkvision with a range of 120 feet.
As an action, you can make an unarmed strike against a creature within 5 feet of the serpent or cause it to return to your arm.

subclassFeature.name: Abyssal Stare
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can reach beyond the darkness. You have truesight with a range of 15 feet, and creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they enter the reach of your unarmed strike, which can only be made with it.

subclassFeature.name: Serpent's Maw
entries: Starting at 14th level, the Night Serpent will consume the world. You create a void on a point you can see. Each creature within 60 feet of that point must make a Constitution saving throw. A creature takes {@dice 8d8} force damage and is pulled 30 feet toward the point on a failed save, or half as much damage and is pulled half as far on a successful one. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, they die and are swallowed by the Night Serpent, and the creature can be restored to life only by means of a true resurrection or a wish spell.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: The Night
entries: You've formed a pact with an embodiment of the night, feared despite their gentleness. While every creature under the veil of darkness wishes for respite, night is the time that they are weakest and most afraid. The knowledge you've been granted is for the purpose of calming that fear, allowing the weary to find a moment of rest within the dark.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Night lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Symphony of the Night
entries: Starting at 1st level, you prepare the party for the dark night with your song. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance} skill.

Additionally, you can perform a lullaby during a short rest. A number of creatures other than you up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) that hear your lullaby can sleep until the end of the rest. A creature that finishes a short rest this way regains 1 spent Hit Die, which it can spend immediately.
Once a creature regains 2 Hit Dice from this feature, it can't benefit from this feature again until it finishes a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Down a Dark Path
entries: Starting at 6th level, night is clear as day. You gain darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet.

As an action, you can reduce the range of your darkvision by 30 feet and cause a creature you touch to gain darkvision out to a range of 60 feet or increase the range of its darkvision by 30 feet. The effect lasts for 1 hour or until you end it (no action required). You can't reduce the range of your darkvision below 0 feet using this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Night's Embrace
entries: Starting at 10th level, you're held closely by the night. When you are damaged by a creature, you can use your reaction to cause magical darkness as with the darkness spell to fill the area within 30 feet of you until the end of your next turn. You and any creature affected by your Down a Dark Path feature can see normally in this darkness. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Starry Night
entries: Starting at 14th level, the countless lights that are scattered across the night sky are on your side. As an action, choose a number of creatures you can see up to twice your Charisma modifier (minimum 2). You and each chosen creature must be under the night sky without any obstruction. If the creature is friendly, it regains {@dice 8d8} hit points from soothing moonlight. If it isn't, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it takes {@dice 8d8} radiant damage and is blinded until the end of its next turn from burning starlight. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't blinded.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Predator
entries: Among barbarians, the Path of the Predator isn't uncommon. Rage is primal ferocity, but they delve beneath even that and become indistinguishable from a beast. With a primal instinct to kill or be killed, many who tread this path tend to die early. Those who are able to survive long enough with their instinct alone reach the apex, they can consider everything else prey.

entries.name: Bloody Beast
entries: Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, the amount of prey doesn't make a difference, you'll hunt them all down. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack while you're raging, you can attempt to damage another creature with the same attack. Choose another creature within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on the weapon's damage dice. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

Additionally, you devour your prey before they escape you. When you roll initiative, you can use a reaction to move up to your speed toward a hostile creature that you can see or hear. You must end this move closer to the enemy than you started.

entries.name: Bloody Feast
entries: At 3rd level, you're never without a way to hunt. Your bite is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.
Additionally, your senses have become accustomed to the scent of blood. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell or taste.

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Stance
entries: Starting at 6th level, you lower yourself to the earth and move with four limbs, the way a beast would move. When you have two free hands, your walking speed increases by 10 feet, and opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Rip the Throat
entries: Starting at 10th level, the lives of any that falter before you are forfeit. When you score a critical hit against a creature, you can use your bonus action to make an unarmed strike using your bite against it. On a hit, the attack is a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: Rule of Nature
entries: At 14th level, your dominance can't be questioned, all are prey in your presence. As an action, you can unleash an immense roar. Each hostile creature within 60 feet of you that can hear you must suceed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. A creature has a speed of 0 and is {@condition incapacitated} while {@condition frightened} in this way. A creature of CR (or level) 3 or lower automatically fails the saving throw. You can't roar again until you finish a long rest.
Additionally, you've slaughtered countless prey. When you damage a creature of CR (or level) 3 or lower with a melee weapon attack, you can reduce the creature's hit points to 0.

subclassFeature.name: Destroyer
entries: The Destroyers are a special type of combatant often found lurking within the depths, or fighting that which could only be deemed as prey, for the sake of protection, truth, power, or sometimes the thrill of battle itself. Their way of fighting is ruthless, and they seek to end their foes in the quickest way possible, be it by choking, grappling, or ripping them in twain.

entries.name: Flesh Ripper
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your hits cut deeper, and the momentum of battle flows through you like a wave. Throughout combat you gain momentum, to a maximum of 10, which can be used to perform Onslaughts. Momentum is gained and lost in the followings ways:

items: You gain 3 momentum when you roll initiative, and at the end of your turn if at least one of your attacks hit.
You lose 2 momentum if you don't attack on your turn.
You lose all momentum when combat ends.

entries.name: Onslaught
entries: At 3rd level, when you take the Attack action on your turn, momentum can be used for the following Onslaughts when you make an attack or hit, as specified in the Onslaught. Damage dealt with an Onslaught is always of the weapon's type; bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. You can use multiple Onslaughts on one attack if you can afford them, but you cannot use the same one multiple times:

entries.name: Harpoon Strike
entries: When you would make a ranged weapon attack with a thrown weapon, you can spend 2 momentum to double the normal range and long range at which the thrown weapon can be used. If you hurl at a target that is within the weapon's normal range, you deal an additional {@dice 3d4} damage on a hit, and the target is pushed 5 feet away from you. As part of the attack to throw the weapon, you can draw another melee weapon.

entries.name: Lunging Jaws
entries: If your movement speed isn't 0, you can spend 2 momentum to make a melee weapon attack against a creature up to 10 feet beyond your reach. If you do so, you move until you're within 5 feet of the creature as part of the attack towards it, and add {@dice 1d6} damage to the damage roll.

entries.name: Mutilate
entries: When you hit with a melee weapon attack against a hostile creature, you can spend 4 momentum to inflict an additional {@dice 3d6} damage and reduce the target's speed by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Destroyer's Grasp
entries: At 7th level, your grip is that of a leviathan's jaws. You gain a bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made to maintain a grapple equal to your Constitution modifier, and your melee attacks count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
Additionally, you gain a swim speed equal to your walking speed. If you already have a swim speed, it instead increases by 10 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Unrelenting Assault
entries: At 10th level, you fight like a relentless current. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to attempt to grapple or shove the target. While grappling a creature, the damage of your unarmed strikes and any melee weapon you use against the grappled creature increases to {@dice 1d8}, unless it is already higher.
Additionally, when you use your Action Surge feature, you gain 3 momentum.

subclassFeature.name: Flesh Destroyer
entries: Starting at 15th level, the waves of battle flow through you with ease. When you roll initiative, you start with an additional 1 momentum for each hostile creature you can see within 120 feet of you beyond the first, and your maximum becomes equal to your fighter level. Additionally, you gain access to the following Onslaughts:

entries.name: Destroy
entries: If you hit a creature with two melee weapon attacks in the same turn, you can spend any amount of momentum to deal {@dice 1d6} damage per momentum spent beyond the first and add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage.

entries.name: Endless Pursuit
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 6 momentum to deal an additional {@dice 5d6} damage and mark it for inevitable doom. Your movement speed can't be reduced until the end of your next turn unless you're incapacitated. At the start of your next turn, you can move up to 120 feet toward the target. You must end this movement within 5 feet of the target if possible.

entries.name: Hammerhead Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 4 momentum to deal an additional {@dice 3d6} damage and push the target up to 30 feet directly away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Rip and Tear
entries: Starting at 18th level, you become a harbinger of death and destruction, tearing the opportunity for survival from your enemies' eyes. When a melee weapon attack by you would reduce a creature to hit points less than your fighter level + your Strength modifier, the creature is reduced to 0 hit points. If the creature was grappled by you, it is instead two times your fighter level + your Strength modifier.
Additionally, when you kill a creature with a melee weapon attack, you gain 2 momentum.

subclassFeature.name: School of Invention
entries: Your study isn't focused, driven instead by inspiration and whatever you're interested in at the moment. An inventor is biased toward experimentation and field work rather than theory and rote repetition. They wear arcane armor of their own design to serve as protection in their desperate search for another breakthrough, another advancement that will alter the way that everyone thinks about the arcane. They work with a controlled chaos that is well known to them but unpredictable to everyone else. The line between brilliance and insanity is narrow, and you cross that line as you wish.

entries.name: Invention Savant
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you gain proficiency in two artisan's tools of your choice, and you add the {@spell find familiar} spell to your spellbook if it is not there already. When you cast {@spell find familiar}, the familiar can instead be a construct.

entries.name: Arcane Armor
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you've developed a precaution for your work. You gain proficiency with light armor, and can create a set of magical armor, called arcane armor, with a ritual. The ritual can be done during a long rest, and uses 10 gp worth of iron or steel and a set of leather or studded leather armor.
Only you can wear the arcane armor you created, and while you're wearing it, you have immunity against damage from your own spells, unless you choose to take the damage.

The armor provides an AC of 13 + your Dexterity modifier, weighs 10 pounds, and can be donned or doffed as an action. If you create another set of arcane armor, any previous set of arcane armor reverts to non-magical armor.

entries.name: Shift Spells
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can introduce a random variable into your own magic to catch the enemy by surprise. When you cast a spell that deals damage, you can roll on the Shift Spells table and substitute the damage type for the result. If the result is the same as the original damage type, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell. You can use this feature twice. You regain expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Idea Improvement
entries: Starting at 6th level, you've developed improvements to your arcane armor, soon enough it could be called a masterpiece. Your arcane armor gains one of the following options.

entries.name: Helm
entries: You can use a bonus action instead of an action to see through the senses of a familiar you summoned with {@spell find familiar}, and you aren't blinded or deafened during that time.

entries.name: Gloves
entries: When you target a creature within 5 feet of you with a ranged spell attack, you can consider it a melee spell attack instead. You can use this feature only once per turn.

entries.name: Boots
entries: You gain a flying speed of 15 feet, you fall if you end your turn in the air and nothing else is holding you aloft.
Your armor gains another option at 10th and 14th level.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Ingenuity
entries: At 10th level, you're able to adapt to the situation rather than work with what you have. You can change a number of spells on your list of prepared spells equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) when you finish a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Peerless Pioneer
entries: At 14th level, you've reached the forefront of the arcane, you will lead the way to the future. When you cast a wizard spell of 1st through 5th level, you can cause that spell to be cast as if cast with a spell slot one level higher. If you do, you cannot cast spells other than cantrips until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, when a creature you can see within 30 feet of you casts a spell, you can cause that spell to be cast as if cast with a spell slot two levels higher as a reaction. You can't use this reaction again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Seer Bloodline
entries: You've inherited both the blood and sight of a being able to see into the future. The being could be an oracle or even a deity, and it's certain your birth has been foretold by them. You can see what destiny has in store for you, but be warned, your destiny is not set in stone. To see the past, present, and future, then decide your own destiny, that is the seer's right.

subclassFeature.name: See the Future
entries: Starting at 1st level, your bloodline allows you to learn spells that allow you to see into the future. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose a divination spell that isn't on the sorcerer spell list. You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you.
At 6th level, when you cast a divination spell of 1st level or higher using a spell slot, you regain sorcery points equal to the level of the spell, up to a maximum of 5.

subclassFeature.name: Second Sight
entries: At 1st level, you can see several seconds ahead. While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier.

Additionally, you predict the future, and any who subvert it are punished by the world itself. As a bonus action, choose a hostile creature you can see within 10 feet of you, and say whether or not the creature will target you with an attack or harmful spell during its next turn. The creature understands your prediction, even if it can't hear you or doesn't know your language. At the end of the creature's next turn, if it didn't act as you predicted, it takes force damage equal to equal to half your sorcerer level (rounded up) + your Charisma modifier.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.

subclassFeature.name: Minute Sight
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can see several minutes ahead, you act before you're acted against. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier.
Additionally, you can spend 1 sorcery point to use your Second Sight without expending a use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Hour Sight
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can see several hours ahead, you act before destiny itself. You can cast divination spells with a casting time of 1 to 10 minutes as an action.
Additionally, you can't be surprised while you're conscious.

subclassFeature.name: Change the Future
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can grasp the future you wish for, even if you suffer for it. Whenever you finish a long rest, note the location. If you die, your corpse and any memory of you since that long rest vanish as if you were never there. Any illogical outcomes of your actions since that long rest are rationalized. For example, if you damaged a creature before you died, the creature rationalizes that another creature damaged it instead. You then appear at the location where you finished your last long rest, alive and in the state you were when you finished the rest.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again for 7 days.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Mad Dog
entries: Those of the Way of the Mad Dog run rampant on the field of battle without restraint or remorse, thirsty for the blood of their enemies. Their mere presence is an omen that those around them will be reduced to gore, they become more feared with each person they murder.

They say when you encounter a Mad Dog, you don't know you're already dead until you've made them bleed. As they lose themselves to their thirst with quaking eyes and a grin that could slice like a knife, your life will flash before your eyes, and you'll regret not writing your will.

entries.name: Mad Dog's Dive
entries: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can tear into the throat of those that think they're safe. Immediately before you take the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to jump 10 feet toward a creature you can see and make a melee weapon attack against the creature as a bonus action. If you hit the creature, it must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.
Additionally, the distance you can leap with a long jump isn't halved when you make a standing long jump.

entries.name: Bloodsoaked Beast
entries: At 3rd level, those who spill your blood will regret it. When a creature hits you with an attack while you have all of your hit points, it must make a Wisdom saving throw against your ki save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, it is immune to this feature for the next 24 hours.
At 11th level, each hostile creature within 60 feet of you must also make a Wisdom saving throw against your ki save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of their next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Mad Dog's Dominance
entries: Starting at 6th level, you keep your enemy under your heel. If a creature within your reach stands from {@condition prone}, you can spend 1 ki point as a reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. If you hit the creature, they're knocked {@condition prone} and their speed is reduced to 0 until the start of their next turn.
Additionally, while you are above half of your hit points, you have advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

subclassFeature.name: Hellfire Heart
entries: Starting at 11th level, the madness within you causes your body to burn with a fire that can only be quenched by blood. When you take the Attack action on your turn and score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack with the action, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action.

subclassFeature.name: Reign
entries: Starting at 17th level, your thirst for blood has become insatiable, those in your path either submit or succumb to you. Using your action, you enter a trance of murderous intent. You gain the following benefits for 10 minutes.

items: You ignore the effects of {@condition exhaustion}, and you cannot gain {@condition exhaustion}.
If you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee weapon attack, you can make another melee weapon attack (no action required by you).
When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the end of its next turn.

entries: When your trance ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Reaper
entries: Those who become a Reaper are aware of a certain truth, which is that any creature born will die. Though many people would say this is common sense, those who truly understand their mortality are rare. Whether they had a near death experience or met a god of death, a Reaper stands at the boundary of life and death. They decide when a creature's time has come.

entries.name: Death Glare
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you understand the fragility of life. You know if the hit points of any creature you can see within 30 feet of you are equal to or below your fighter level + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

Additionally, you can extinguish life with a glance. As an action, choose a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If it has hit points equal to your fighter level + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) or fewer, it is reduced to 0 hit points. Whenever you use this action, your eyes glow for an instant.

subclassFeature.name: Decapitate
entries: Starting at 7th level, you grant an end without agony. When you take the Attack action and a melee weapon attack that you make as part of it hits a creature, you can forgo any number of your attacks from it. For each attack that you forgo, you deal extra damage equal to the weapon's damage + the ability modifier used for the attack. If this leaves the target with 0 hit points, you can cut off or destroy one of the target's heads if possible.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sudden Death
entries: Starting at 10th level, those you see perish without warning. The range of your Death Glare increases to 60 feet, and you can extinguish life using your Death Glare as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Certain Death
entries: Starting at 15th level, you are as inescapable as death itself. You can use a bonus action to mark a creature you can see within 120 feet of you, and teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of it. On each of your turns while you can see the marked creature, you can teleport in this way again using your bonus action. The mark lasts for 1 minute, and it ends early if your turn ends and you haven't attacked the creature since your last turn. When the marked creature dies, you can apply the mark to a different creature you can see within 120 feet of you, provided you aren't {@condition incapacitated}.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: The End
entries: Starting at 18th level, you cross the boundary. Having 0 hit points doesn't knock you unconscious. You still must make death saving throws, and you suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points. If you die and your body isn't destroyed, such as by the disintegrate spell, you can act as if you're alive for a number of your turns equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 turn). You can't act as if you're alive again until you're returned to life and you finish a long rest.
Additionally, whenever you roll damage for a weapon attack while you're dead or have 0 hit points, you can deal maximum damage, instead of rolling.

subclassFeature.name: Servant
entries: There is an endless demand for the skilled, and you're one of them. Regardless of your past, you've decided your countless talents are best used for the sake of someone else. A rogue of this archetype has taken a path of servitude, supporting the person they chose as their master with elegance and grace. Whether you're serving your master tea or murdering their enemies, to do what they're told is the delight of a Servant.

entries.name: Sharply Dressed
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your appearance conceals your lethality. While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 12 + your Dexterity modifier, or 13 + your Dexterity modifier instead if you are wearing fine clothes.

Additionally, you can conceal up to two daggers or any objects of a similiar size in your clothes. While concealed, ability checks made to find the objects have disadvantage, and both can be drawn with a single object interaction.

entries.name: By Your Side
entries: At 3rd level, you stand at your master's side. As a bonus action, choose a friendly creature within 5 feet of you. Until the start of your next turn, whenever the creature moves or is moved, you move to stay within 5 feet of it. If an attacker you can see hits the creature with an attack, you can use your reaction to cause the attack to hit you instead. The effect ends if you choose to end it (no action required), if the creature moves to a location you can't reach, if you move more than twice your movement speed, or if your speed is reduced to 0.
When you gain the Uncanny Dodge feature, you can use it as part of the same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Gentle Care
entries: Starting at 9th level, you tend to your master's health. As an action, you can spend one use of a healer's kit to allow a creature within 5 feet of you to spend one Hit Die. The creature rolls the die, adds its Constitution modifier, and regains a number of hit points equal to the total (minimum of 1). The creature can't regain hit points from this feature again until it finishes a short or long rest.
Starting at 13th level, tending to your master bolsters your spirit. You gain temporary hit points equal to the amount the creature regained.

subclassFeature.name: Prepare Item
entries: Starting at 13th level, you answer your master's whims. If you have a pack or a similar container, you can withdraw any non-magical object worth up to 50 gp that would fit inside, even if you don't have the object. If the object is a dagger or an object of a similiar size, you can instead withdraw the object from your clothes. You must spend an amount of gp equal to the cost of the object to withdraw it. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: By Your Wish
entries: Starting at 17th level, you grant your master's wishes. After you use your By Your Side feature, the first time the chosen creature makes an attack, you can immediately make a weapon attack against the same target. If this attack benefits from your Sneak Attack, the extra damage from it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Monster
entries: A person can't escape death, and neither can they escape the Path of the Monster. You advance slowly and surely toward your enemy, their futile resistance unable to make you falter even a step. The fear they feel from the bottom of their heart is brought to rest by your weapon, another corpse for the pile.

entries.name: Black Iron Body
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you are feared for your body that will not break. You can forgo resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage when you enter a rage. If you do so, you gain temporary hit points equal to your barbarian level + half your current hit points, which vanish when your rage ends or you enter a rage. You can't rage in this way again for 1 minute.

Additionally, after a creature hits you with an attack while you're raging, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or become {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn. If you have all of your hit points, the creature has disadvantage on the saving throw.
Finally, when you roll initiative and you aren't surprised, you can use your reaction to immediately enter a rage.

subclassFeature.name: White Iron Will
entries: Starting at 6th level, you are feared for your will that will not waver. While you're raging and you have all of your hit points, you have advantage on Wisdom saving throws, and you are immune to being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

subclassFeature.name: Sword Iron Soul
entries: Starting at 10th level, you are feared for your soul that will not shatter. On your turn (no action required), you can reduce your hit point maximum to an amount equal to your current hit points until you finish a long rest. If you do so, when you roll a weapon's damage dice until the end of your turn, you can deal maximum damage, instead of rolling.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Rule of Nature
entries: Starting at 14th level, you are a fear that will never fade away, you're considered a monster of myth. While you're raging and you have all of your hit points, you gain the following features.

entries.name: Monster of the Mountain
entries: As a bonus action, you can deal {@dice 6d6} bludgeoning damage to a creature you're grappling or hurl it up to 60 feet in any direction except for upward.

entries.name: Monster of the Plain
entries: As a bonus action, you can make a melee weapon attack against a creature that damaged you since the end of your last turn.

entries.name: Monster of the Sky
entries: As a bonus action, you can fly up to your speed. You fall if you end your turn in the air and nothing else is holding you aloft.

subclassFeature.name: The Shadow
entries: Your patron is a being that is likely viewed in a light of villainy; a Wraith, a Shadow Demon, a particularly potent Shadow, a Nightwalker, or even an embodiment or deity of Darkness itself. Regardless of their point of existence, your pact with that being is one that leaves you with a feeling of fulfilment. You might have ade it as a small child who sought nothing more than a guide through the scary darkness. Or perhaps you made it to have a reliable assistant through your endeavors. You might have even made the pact to have a stalwart companion who would never betray you, or maybe you just wanted to not be alone any more. But the same gnawing question eventually arises within your mind: Why did your patron show you what seems to be kindness? Are they truly benevolent? Have they nefarious goals, as most do? The question of why a kindness was done unto you is one all who make a pact with a patron of shadow ask themselves, but even through the darkest nights and the most insatiable of curiosities, the bestowed shadow stands at your side.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Shadow lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Tenebrous Companion
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can manifest a blessing from your patron in your own shadow at the end of a short or long rest. This shadowy creature resembles you in form, but looks more akin to a creature wrapped in shadows. In appearance you can choose how it looks, but if it resembles an existing creature, they are clearly distinguishable from each other.
