It is friendly to you and your companions, and it obeys your commands. It is sentient, but its personality and alignment match your own, unless your DM decides otherwise. Additionally, you can cast spells as though you are in the shadow's space. See this creature's statistics in the bestowed shadow stat block.

In combat, the bestowed shadow shares your initiative count, but takes its turn immediately after yours. You can command it on its turn (no action required). If you don't, it will follow whichever orders it was given last, or take the Dodge action if those orders have been fulfilled or if it hasn't been given orders.

The bestowed shadow cannot benefit from a short or long rest if you do not also benefit from it. When it completes a short or long rest, it regains all lost hit points. If the bestowed shadow dies, it becomes a part of your shadow once more, and when it is summoned again, it reappears with the memories it had when it was last with you.

subclassFeature.name: Enter the Shadow
entries: Starting at 6th level, your shadows protect you, and you have grown to seek comfort in them. As an action, or as a reaction when an attack hits or misses you, you can teleport to a location of your choosing that you can see within 5 feet of your shadow. If your shadow is unavailable, you can teleport to any location within dim light or darkness that you can see within 120 feet of you. Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Additionally, your bestowed shadow gains a swim speed equal to ten times your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Light Creates Shadow
entries: Starting at 10th level, you embrace light, for without it your companion cannot exist. You have resistance to radiant damage, and when you take radiant damage, your bestowed shadow gains temporary hit points equal to the hit points you lost until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Lonely Eclipse
entries: Starting at 14th level, not only have you embraced the shadows, you have learned to warp it to cause your enemies to feel the loneliness you once did. As an action, you can summon a perfect black sphere around you or your bestowed shadow in a 60-foot radius, which lasts until the end of your next turn. While this sphere is active, the area becomes difficult terrain for hostile creatures, and is a zone of magical darkness. You and your allies can see through this darkness.

When this eclipse appears, all hostile creatures within 60 feet of the source must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw. A creature that fails the saving throw is incapacitated with a speed of 0, and can see and hear only itself, and the darkness around them for the duration. Also when the eclipse appears, you and your bestowed shadow can meld into the shadows and teleport to unoccupied spaces of your choice within the sphere. If you teleported into the air with nothing holding you aloft, you remain there until the sphere disappears, at which point you descend safely. Alternatively, you can spend 5 feet of movement to descend safely. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you complete a long rest.
Additionally, your bestowed shadow gains a fly speed equal to ten times your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Divinity Domain
entries: While a true cleric is a rare enough sight already, a cleric of the Divinity domain might be seen once in a lifetime at most. A cleric of this domain isn't a cleric in the strictest sense, but a godly being able to manifest miracles with their own power, rather than the power of another god. Whether they have the blood of a deity within them or are the incarnation of a deity, they'll become a deity themselves eventually. Until that time has come, they have the freedom to decide their own destiny. That freedom is a blessing granted only to a mortal, not a god.

entries.caption: Divinity Domain Spells
rows: {@spell aura of purity}, {@spell guardian of faith}

subclassFeature.name: Light of Judgement
entries: At 1st level, you judge as a god would. You can use your action to launch a beam of light at a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. The target takes radiant damage equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier, and until the end of your next turn, you can use this feature against the target even if you can't see it. The beam of light ignores cover, but fails if the target isn't within range. You can use this feature only once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: White Plume
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to unleash your divine power. As an action, you can cause two angelic wings to sprout from your back for 1 minute or until you lose your concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell). Any clothing or armor you're wearing are magically altered for the duration to accommodate the angelic wings.
While you have these wings, you gain a flying speed of 30 feet, and you can use Light of Judgement as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Paradise Lost
entries: Starting at 6th level, you rise toward godhood. When you use White Plume, you can spend additional uses of your Channel Divinity. You gain two more wings for each additional use you spend. You also gain two more wings if you use White Plume while you have wings from it. If you have more than two wings from White Plume, it doesn't require concentration.
While you have four wings from White Plume, your flying speed increases to 60 feet, and the damage of Light of Judgement increases by half your cleric level.
While you have six wings from White Plume, your flying speed increases to 120 feet, and the damage of Light of Judgement increases by your cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Endless Light
entries: At 17th level, you're able to wield the power of a god. You can target up to six creatures with Light of Judgement, and the damage increases to 2 + twice your Wisdom modifier.
In addition, when you successfully use Divine Intervention, you can cast any spell instantaneously without expending a spell slot and without components instead of an intervention.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Branch Reader
entries: Within the world, there are endless possibilities. Among the Durumir, famed for their intellect and cosmic connection, there are a gifted few that are able to perceive and act on these possibilities. These are called branches by the Durumir, and those who percieve them are known as branch readers. Rather than be overcome by emotion, they enter a state of ocus and choose one path to take from countless choices.

entries.name: Restriction: Durumir Only
entries: Only durumir can follow the Path of the Branch Reader. To be a branch reader requires a particular sensitivity, and is an honored role rarely bestowed on those of another race. Your DM can lift this restriction to better suit the campaign. It might not apply to your DM's setting.

entries.name: Branching Path
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can read the branches of the great tree. You can forgo resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage when you enter a rage. If you do so, you gain the following benefits for the duration of the rage.

items: Attack rolls made against you have disadvantage.
You have advantage on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws.

When you or a creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by half your barbarian level + your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).
Your rage doesn't end early as a result of not attacking a hostile creature or taking damage since your last turn.

entries.name: Chosen Path
entries: At 3rd level, you decide on your path without any hesitation. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Break the Branch
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can forcefully break a branch that would lead to catastrophe. When you or a creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack while you're under the effect of Branching Path, you can cause your rage to end early and reduce the damage to 0. You can't use this feature again for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Prepare the Path
entries: Starting at 10th level, you prepare the path for everyone else. Immediately before the start of your turn, you can exchange the initiative count of you and a willing creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you. The target acts immediately, and you act on your new initative count.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Knowledge of the Great Tree
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can reach a state of enlightenment, able to read the branches while you're overcome by emotion. When you enter a rage, you can benefit from Branching Path without forgoing resistance.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Legion
entries: There is a layer of the Abyss within which nature is constantly absorbing itself, in order to grow and perpetuate. The everconsuming force is alluring to some, and encapsulating to others. They become so in tune with the feeling and need for perpetual consumption that they extend it to the nature of the Material Plane, and even to the monsters they summon, eventually able to harness the power of their souls and expel them with great force.

entries.name: Circle Spells
entries: Your understanding of the energies of the Abyss grants you the eternal knowledge to cast spells sometimes beyond your grasp. At 2nd level, you learn the {@spell primal savagery|XGE} cantrip. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to the circle spells listed below.

Once you gain access to a circle spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.

entries.name: Abyssal Wilds
entries: Starting at 2nd level, the chaotic energies of the abyss flow through your veins, and manifest at your will when you transform. When you expend a use of wild shape to take a new form, you gain temporary hit points equal to your druid level + your Wisdom modifier, and can change the damage type of the new form's attacks to cold or fire for the duration, chosen when you transform. While transformed in this way, you can appear as a demonic mockery of your chosen form bristling with the element you chose, changing your type to fiend for the duration.
Additionally, when you expend a use of wild shape to take a new form, you can cast your {@spell primal savagery|XGE} cantrip even if you normally couldn't cast it in that form.

subclassFeature.name: Consume
entries: At 6th level, your knowledge expands to fiendish depths, and you can consume demons you summon to overhaul your capabilities. When you summon one or more demons with a spell, you can expend a use of wild shape to merge with one as part of the action used to cast the spell. If you do, you must sacrifice that creature as you cast the spell, reducing it to 0 hit points. For the duration of the spell, you gain the ability scores, armor class, saving throw proficiencies, languages, resistances, immunities, movement speeds, traits, actions, and reactions that the creature you summon would have, unless your own would be better. Additionally, you gain temporary hit points equal to the creature's hit point maximum, and you can choose to change your size to match the creature's own. While morphed in this way, you follow the wild shape restrictions for spellcasting, and your damage is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks and damage.
If you are under the effects of Abyssal Wilds, you can cast a spell to activate Consume even if you normally couldn't cast it in that form.

subclassFeature.name: Fiendish Regeneration
entries: At 10th level, you can destroy a monster you've conjured to fuel yourself. You can slay a creature you have summoned or created as an action to regain a number of hit points equal to the creature's current hit points. You cannot exceed half your hit point maximum from healing in this way.

subclassFeature.name: Heart of Chaos
entries: At 14th level, the Abyss has buried deep into your heart, enabling even greater capabilities. When you cast a spell to activate Consume, it doesn't require concentration, and you can use one of the creature's attacks as a bonus action on the same turn you cast the spell.

Additionally, you can cast spells while under the effects of Consume if the merged creature had the Innate Spellcasting trait, but only to cast spells from its list. You cast it using your spell slots, and the spell is considered a Druid spell for you.
You can also cast one spell from its list without expending a spell slot while merged. Once you cast a spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ysgardian Bloodline
entries: The forefathers were known for their brutality and inspiring strength on the battlefield, and when they fell, their spirits ascended to the halls of Ysgard. However, their spirits did not go without a fight, and each of them left behind a portion of their essence for their descendants to one day tap into, to protect them, and to perhaps one day fight with them. Perhaps your ancestor was a powerful viking, or perhaps this essence was passed onto you from an ended bloodline by the Allfather, or another being; maybe even the spirit of a dead viking themselves bestowed this unto you. But any sorcerer, be it by birthright or by shouldering the burden of another lineage that met its demise, can tap into the powers of Ysgard.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: You gain proficiency with simple melee weapons, martial melee weapons, and light armor.

subclassFeature.name: Spirits of the Halls
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can call forth the spirits of your ancestors to repel your foes, or to aid you in dire straits. As a bonus action, you can summon two Ysgardian spirits, which hover in your space, to fight alongside you. These spirits last for 1 minute, or until you use this feature again. Your carrying capacity is multiplied by an amount equal to 1 + the number of spirits you currently have. You can command the spirits to act in the following ways:

items: When you deal damage to a creature, you can command one or more spirits to push that creature 15 feet in a direction of your choice except directly upwards, plus an extra 15 feet for every spirit you command past the first.
Whenever you make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to command one or more spirits to aid you. You can roll {@dice 1d4} per spirit you command and add it to the result of the saving throw.

entries: After a spirit acts, it disappears. You can use this ability three times, and regain all expended uses on a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Fury of Ysgard
entries: At 6th level, the Ysgardian spirits you summon brandish weapons with which to strike your foes, their legacies fighting through you. When you use Spirits of the Halls, you gain an additional command option:

items: As a bonus action, you can command one spirit to strike a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The spirit uses your spell attack modifier for attack rolls and deals force damage equal to {@dice 1d12} + your Charisma modifier on a hit. Alternatively, you can use an action to command any number of spirits remaining to attack, rolling separately for each spirit.

entries: Additionally, if you have no uses remaining of Spirits of the Halls, you can use it by spending up to 3 sorcery points to summon that many spirits.

subclassFeature.name: Ysgardian Ancestry
entries: At 14th level, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can call upon the immortality of your ancestors. You are reduced to 1 hit point instead, and your hit points can't fall below 1 until after the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Additionally, you know the {@spell plane shift} spell, it doesn't count against your spells known, and you don't need a tuning fork to travel to Ysgard. Traveling to Ysgard makes you arrive at the gates of Valhalla.

subclassFeature.name: Immortal Legacy
entries: At 18th level, your glory becomes eternal, and the legacy of your ancestors has become immortalized. Memories of you can never be erased from a creature's mind, and you are always a valid target for the {@spell legend lore} spell.
Ysgardian spirits no longer disappear when you summon more. Instead, you can have up to five Ysgardian spirits active at once, and they last until you finish a long rest.
Additionally, you gain five Ysgardian spirits whenever you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Royalty Domain
entries: You serve a deity of royalty, often rulers of pantheons such as Odin, Zeus, or Ba'al, who supports sovereignty by divine right to rule. You are an emissary of this belief; often dealing with the issues and strife caused between nobility and the common folk, ensuring the lessers know their place, and also preventing corruption and abuse by those above them.

subclassFeature.name: Monarch's Presence
entries: At 1st level, you gain proficiency with heavy armor and the Performance skill.
Additionally, you can use your bonus action to summon a spiritual symbol of your station, such as a crown, emblem, or ring, which remains until you dismiss it or summon it again.

subclassFeature.name: Royal Decree
entries: Also at 1st level, you can emit a royal presence causing others to falter. As an action, you cause other creatures within 10 feet of you, except those you choose, to have disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of your next turn.
As a reaction when a creature under this effect misses you with an attack, you can make an attack against it.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Crown's Guard
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to call upon your deity for protection befitting the crown.
As a reaction to taking damage, you can present your holy symbol and use your Channel Divinity to reduce the damage you take by {@dice 1d10} + your Wisdom modifier + your cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: Imposing Exile
entries: At 6th level, you can expel transgressors, causing them to stagger backwards from your brilliance. When you damage a creature with a weapon attack or cleric cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet directly away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: Starting at 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with your force of will. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} force damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@dice 2d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Sovereign Immunity
entries: At 17th level, none can harm you, for all are undeserving to strike you. You are always under the effect of the {@spell sanctuary} spell, using your spell save DC. If a creature succeeds a saving throw against it, it's immune to this effect for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Avenger
entries: While many barbarians channel their rage from a well of anger sculpted by focus, experience, or pushing themselves beyond their limits, those of the Path of the Avenger channel their rage from a deep sense of regret, and a desire for atonement for a mistake they cannot undo. Extremely vengeful and known to traverse entire planes of existence to slay those who have wronged their allies, they are ruthless, protective, and to those foolish enough to cross them, a nightmare made manifest.

entries.name: Roar of Resolve
entries: Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, your vengeful roar bolsters your allies. When you rage, you can choose up to five friendly creatures other than you that hear this roar within 30 feet of you. These creatures gain temporary hit points equal to your barbarian level.
At 6th level, you can repeat this vengeful roar when you use your bonus action to intentionally end your rage.

entries.name: Avenger's Fury
entries: Also at 3rd level, your fury brings forth memories of the fallen, and the weapons they wielded. While you're raging, an ethereal retributive weapon floats above your head, taking an appearance of your choosing. When a friendly creature is hit by an attack from a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, and the damage reduces the temporary hit points from your Roar of Resolve feature to 0, you can use your reaction to send the retributive weapon to the attacker. The target takes force damage equal to {@dice 1d8} + your rage damage bonus + your barbarian level, and the retributive weapon returns to you. If the attacker is more than 5 feet away from you, you only add half your barbarian level to the damage.
Additionally, while raging you are aware of the location of any friendly creature within 120 feet of you that is dying or at 0 hit points, even if it is invisible.

entries.name: A Lesson Learned
entries: Also at 3rd level, you have learned from your mistakes. You automatically succeed Wisdom (Medicine) checks made to stabilize a dying creature, and you can stabilize a creature as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Reckless Abandon
entries: Starting at 6th level, you swing with reckless abandon to distract your foes from those under your charge. When you hit a creature with an attack after using your Reckless Attack feature, the creature has disadvantage on attacks targeting creatures that have temporary hit points from your Roar of Resolve feature until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Avenger's Eye
entries: Starting at 10th level, those soaked in the blood of your allies will never escape your wrath. When a hostile creature damages any creature friendly to you, you are aware of its location if it is within 30 feet of you, even if it is hidden or invisible, until the end of your next turn.

You can also track those that have fled from their crime. You can spend 1 minute concentrating on a wound a creature friendly to you has been dealt. Until your next long rest, you know how far the creature that dealt the wound is and in what direction it lies. Whenever you are presented with a choice of paths along the way, you automatically determine which path is the shortest and most direct (but not necessarily the safest) route to the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Unrelenting Vengeance
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can command the weapons of the fallen even when resolve is weakest to ensure none will fall. You can send your retributive weapon towards a creature that hits a friendly creature even if it wasn't benefiting from your Roar of Resolve feature.

Additionally, when an ally is reduced to 0 hit points by a hostile creature while you're raging, your next attack against that creature before the end of your next turn automatically hits and is a critical hit. You can gain this benefit only once per rage.
